tips.englishNotes: Magic Lamp is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: ETG Shooting an enemy causes a mote of blue energy (a wish) to appear above their head. XTG Shooting an enemy summons a Gunie to punch them for large damage. Each shot summons a gunie, with no need to accumulate three wishes. Notes: The Ammonomicon entry is a reference to the Disney film Aladdin, where one of the rules of the Genie character was that he won't kill anybody. The Sweetness and Light synergy is a reference to an English idiom. ETG The Magic Lamp has the distinction of being item ID 0 in the Enter the Gungeon loot database.

Winchester is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. It is similar to the Regular Shotgun, and has a higher fire rate, lower reload time, and less spread, but less max ammo. Notes: The Winchester is wielded by Blue Shotgun Kin. The quote "Better Than A Box Of Roses" is a reference to a scene in the movie Terminator 2: Judgement Day, where the Terminator pulls a Winchester out of a box full of roses. When reloading the Winchester, the player will spin the weapon backwards around the trigger hand. This is in imitation of what the Terminator does in the movie.

Thompson Sub-Machinegun is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: ETG The Thompson Sub-Machinegun is wielded by Hollowpoints. "Myeah, See!" references a line spoken by Edward G. Robinson's gangster character in the 1931 film Little Caesar. This gun is used in The Convict's past.

Screecher is a gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Rapidly fires piercing sound waves that grow in width the further they travel. Waves have a 1% chance to stun enemies for 3 seconds on impact. Notes: The sound waves destroy chests very quickly; it is not recommended to check for mimics using this gun. The sound waves also crack the walls of secret rooms very quickly, making it a great, ammo efficient gun for locating them. The gun could be a reference to anti-riot weapons such as the Long Range Acoustic Device (LRAD), which uses a form of non-kinetic and non-lethal form of crowd dispersal.

Sticky Crossbow is a gun that fires bolts which stick to enemies. Upon reloading, the bolts explode. Bolts may also be detonated by switching weapons at any time. Notes: Triple Sticky - If the player also has Triple Crossbow, Sticky Crossbow will fire three bolts per shot. Triple Crossbow's triple shot will explode upon hitting an enemy. If the player has a passive allowing bullets to pierce, the explosion will occur where the bolt struck. Exploding bolts will ignite pools of oil. Trivia: This gun's behaviour and description is a reference to the AGL grenade launcher from the game Teleglitch. This gun is used in The Hunter's past.

The A.W.P. is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It fires bullets that can pierce through one enemy. As a sniper rifle, its shot speed is extremely fast, and its shots leave lines of smoke. Notes: Due to its slow rate of fire it is easy to fire another gun between A.W.P. shots without A.W.P. downtime. A.W.P is an acronym for Arctic Warfare Police. "Noob Cannon" is a reference to the AWP weapon in Counter-Strike, popular among new and experienced players alike for its ability to one-shot opposing players.

Zorgun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Rapidly fires homing (Rad: 5 Vel: 600) projectiles. The last shot of each magazine fires either an RPG rocket, a Grenade Launcher grenade, or a Bullet Bore drill. Holding down the fire button will not automatically fire the last round, the button must be let go and pressed again. Notes: The Zorgun is a reference to the ZF1 weapon sold by the villain Zorg in the film The Fifth Element. The entry for the Zorgun in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of a strangely dressed Bullet Kin and a tablet styled after a Shotgun Kin.

Barrel is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires blue fish. ETG Fish have a 20% chance to Stun (Dur: 2.5s) enemies, and leave pools of water when flopping onto the ground that are capable of extinguishing chest fuses and the fireplace. Notes: The gun itself as well as its flavor text are a reference to the idiom "Like shooting fish in a barrel" describing an effortless or simple action, with guaranteed success. One Fish Two Fish Is a clear reference to a book of a similar name written by Dr. Seuss. This gun's quote in Portuguese, "Isso, Isso, Isso", is a reference to a popular Mexican TV show called El Chavo del Ocho about a boy who lives in a barrel.

Bow is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Charges to shoot arrows. If fired fully charged, its arrows will move faster, deal more damage, and pierce enemies. Notes: Arrowkin wield the Bow. The charge effect is likely a reference to the game "Titan Souls". It could also be a reference to the Light Arrow from The Legend of Zelda games.

Dueling Pistol is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires large, piercing musket balls which bounce twice. Notes: The Hell Singing synergy is likely a reference to Van Helsing, the vampire hunter created by Bram Stoker. The Dead Prez synergy is likely a reference to the death of Abraham Lincoln, who was shot with a Derringer, and the Dueling Pistol likely is referencing the many duels that Andrew... The entry for the Dueling Pistol in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of one musketball gundead shooting another dead with the weapon.

Mega Douser is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: The Mega Douser fires a continuous stream of water with strong knockback but relatively low damage. This stream leaves water goop on the ground beneath it at it travels. This water stream can extinguish the fireplace, put out Chest fuses, and clean off hidden Rainbow Chests. Because the Mega Douser uses the Water damage type, it deals 2x damage to Muzzle Wisps and Muzzle Flares, and does not ignite Coalers. Notes: The name and appearance of the gun reference Super Soaker, a brand of toy water guns. More specifically, its shape and color scheme are based on the original Super Soaker 50.

The Crossbow is a gun in Enter the Gungeon that fires a single high-damage bolt. The Hunter starts with this gun. Notes: There is a delay between the shot being fired and the auto reload which can be canceled with a manual reload. If playing in coop mode and the Hunter dies whilst holding the Crossbow, it will disappear. It will not disappear if others hold it. The entry for the Crossbow in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of the weapon and Junior II, referencing the Hunter's starting loadout.

Thunderclap is a gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Quickly fires small balls of electricity. ETG Any pools of water that these balls pass over will be electrified, dealing considerable damage to enemies. This gun does not grant electricity immunity. As such, electrified pools of water also pose considerable danger to the player. Notes: Altered: Diazepam - Prior to the Advanced Gungeons & Draguns Update, this gun was included in Diazepam. The damaging field while reloading was added in A Farewell to Arms. This weapon's appearance is a reference to the Thunderbolt weapon from the video game series Quake and the Plasma Gun from Doom.

Bee Hive is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires bees that home (Rad: 10 Vel: 150) in on and damage enemies. The initial 3 damage impact of each bee is affected by the players damage stat, but the lingering swarm damage is not. Notes: ETG If the player feeds the Bee Hive and the RPG to a regular Muncher, it will always spit out a Stinger. ETG Bees will not home in on enemies that are not necessary for room clear. ETG Bees caught by Gunjurers retain their quick movement and tight homing, making them extrmely hard for the player to dodge.

AK-47 is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon, Exit the Gungeon, and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. In the Apple Arcade version of Exit the Gungeon, the AK-47 is called the AP-13. Notes: The AK-47 is wielded by some Bullet KinETG, TankersETG, and HollowpointsXTG. ETG If the player feeds the AK-47 and either the Flame Hand or Megahand to a regular Muncher, it will always spit out a Machine Fist. The description of the AK-47 is a reference to the film Jackie Brown.

Yari Launcher is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Rapidly fires tightly homing (Rad: Vel: 220) rockets that create small explosions upon hitting an enemy. The damage dealt by these rockets (both their impact damage and explosive damage) bypass the Boss DPS cap, allowing this gun to kill bosses extremely quickly. Notes: The gun is a reference to the rocket launcher in the 2013 reboot of Shadow Warrior. The orbiting Demon Head gained through the Shadow Warrior synergy is likely a reference to one of the game's weapons, a demon head that shoots a laser out of its mouth.

Heck Blaster is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Rapidly fires invisible bullets. Jammed enemies and bosses hit by the Heck Blaster will revert to normal. Notes: This gun is useful in high Curse runs, as it gives players a counter to the high spawn rates of Jammed enemies. Gunjurers cannot absorb Heck Blaster's bullets. This gun is also capable of reverting Jammed bosses to normal, including the Jammed Lich that appears in the Glitched Lich fight.

Blooper is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a shotgun blast of 6 short-range projectiles with high knockback. Deals 20% less damage to bosses. Notes: Gunjurers cannot catch the Blooper's shots. Blooper is a reference to the 2012 time travel film Looper. The gun's tagline, "Close Your Bloop", is a reference to the movie's tagline, "Close Your Loop". The entry for the Blooper in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of a Bullet Kin executing another in a field of grain, using the weapon.

Grenade Launcher is a gun in Enter the Gungeon, Exit the Gungeon, and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. Effects: Fires explosive grenades. ETG Grenades skip across the ground, and are able to ricochet off of a wall or object once. Notes: This gun is based on the real-world M79 Grenade Launcher, which was developed as a higher powered and more accurate substitute for the US Military's rifle-barrel grenades. The item quote "Fwomp!" is a reference to the real-life weapon's distinctive report when fired. Grenade Launcher's max ammo was increased from 25 to 60 in A Farewell to Arms.

Moonscraper is a gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a laser beam that reflects off of walls or obstacles once. Notes: This gun is a reference to the Moonraker laser rifle from the 1979 James Bond movie Moonraker, as well as the Moonraker Laser weapon from the Nintendo 64 game, GoldenEye 007. The synergy Land Among The Stars references the idiom, "Shoot for the moon; even if you miss, you’ll land among the stars." The synergy Jumping...

BSG is a gun that slowly charges up to fire a large green projectile that, upon reaching its maximum distance, will detonate and severely damage all enemies in the room. Notes: Items that increase shot speed (such as Rocket-Powered Bullets) will allow projectiles to reach their maximum distance quicker and thus detonate in less time. Projectiles will detonate instantly if they come in contact with a black hole created by either the Black Hole Gun or Singularity. The explosion's effect is identical to that of the Bracket Key, with the exception that it does not stop additional enemy waves from spawning; it can damage certain bosses during...

Shades's Revolver is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Increases Coolness by 3 while in the players inventory, decreasing the cooldown of active items and increasing the chance of items dropping upon clearing a room. Notes: The pickup line "Someone Loses An Eye", could be a reference to the latter part of the phrase "It's all fun and games until someone loses an eye." The corresponding pickup line of Smiley's Revolver, "All Smiles," is likely a corruption of the first part of the phrase. The "notches" referenced in the Ammonomicon Entry are usually indicative of a soldier's confirmed kills. As such, it is implied that Shades has killed 17 Gungeoneers.

Dungeon Eagle is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It can be charged to fire a more powerful shot, or tap fired to shoot more frequent, weaker projectiles. Notes: The name Dungeon Eagle is a reference to the real-world 'Desert Eagle' gun. The description of the weapon refers to the Desert Eagle's popularity in American culture and the weapon's iconic reputation despite its shortcomings. With Don't Hoot the Messenger, the reload animation of the gun shows its slide and barrel rotating 360 degrees in reference to the real-world ability of owls to rotate their heads...

The Dart Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It serves as The Cultists starting gun in both games. Notes: The Dart Gun may be a reference to the Nerf Maverick REV-6, a toy foam dart blaster that has the same shape, similar color pattern (albeit purple instead of yellow), and also hold... The Dart Gun is a replacement toy for the Cultist, after they lost their previous toy blaster (the Light Gun) down a pit. The alternate skin of this gun resembles a worm, in order to match the Cultists apple alt skin.

M1 is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. In the Apple Arcade version of Exit the Gungeon, the M1 is called the Grand Rifle. Effects: Bullets from the M1 pierce through enemies and decorations, and travel at an extremely high speed. On reload, the M1 makes a distinctive "ping" sound. Notes: Although classified as semi-automatic, the M1 is functionally automatic, as its rate of fire does not benefit from tapping the fire button instead of holding it down. There are several real guns with the name 'M1', however the shape (such as the band around the rifle's forend), distinctive pinging reload sound, and Ammonomicon entry of the weap...

Nail Gun is a gun in Enter the Gungeon that fires a rapid stream of low damage nails. Notes: This gun is among those Gunsling King and Manservantes can challenge the player to clear a room with. The Ammonomicon entry is a reference to the 1990s horror film Arachnophobia. This weapon may also be a reference to Home Improvement: Power Tool Pursuit!, as the character in that game can also wield a nail gun, which shares the inaccuracy, fire rate, and...

The Light Gun is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires an incredibly fast zap of white light capable of piercing through enemies. The final bullet in each magazine is a mallard duck, which automatically seeks out enemies to attack them. This duck is capable of piercing once, and has extremely tight homing. Like all other piercing projectiles, the duck's damage will halve after piercing an enemy for the first time. Notes: This gun is a reference to the NES Zapper. The duck being shot is a reference to Duck Hunt, one of the most well-known games to use the NES Zapper.

Mailbox is a gun that fires mail at enemies, with the final shot in each magazine being a package. The package can explode, leave glitter, burn, or poison enemies. Notes: This gun is the only way to get the Day Ruiner achievement, acquired by killing a boss after covering it in glitter. Many of the final shot's effects are references to real mail-related attacks: The option of a box filled with glitter is a reference to postal glitter bombs.

VertebraeK-47 is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Rapidly fires homing ghost-shaped bullets that can pierce once. When close together, these bullets form chain links between each other that damage enemies. ETG Each chain deals 2 damage to enemies it overlaps. Enemies hurt by a chain have a 1 second cooldown before they can be harmed by the same chain again. Notes: The weapon's name is a portmanteau of 'vertebrae' and 'AK-47'. Vertebrae are bones which a spine consists of, and the AK-47 is an assault rifle. The description of the gun is a reference to the Jake Clover game, Nuign Spectre.

M1911 is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: This gun is one of the six guns wielded by the Beholster. As it is held by the Beholster, it can be placed on the Beholster Shrine. The in-game description erroneously refers to the M1911's magazine as a clip.

Klobbe is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It rapidly fires inaccurate, low-damage bullets. Notes: This gun is among those Gunsling King and Manservantes can challenge the player to clear a room with. This gun is a reference to the Klobb from GoldenEye 007 (itself based on the real-life Škorpion), a notoriously bad gun with similar weaknesses as the Klobbe. The Klobbering Time synergy is a reference to Fantastic Four member The Thing, whose catchphrase is "It's clobbering time".

Void Marshal is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It fires fast-moving laser projectiles. Notes: This gun is one of the six guns wielded by the Beholster. As it is held by the Beholster, it can be placed on the Beholster Shrine.

Tear Jerker is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Shoots tears that pierce enemies and leave puddles of water on collision. The direction of these tears is affected by the player's momentum, meaning that tears angle in the direction the player is moving. Notes: Because the Tear Jerker uses the Water damage type, it deals 2x damage to Muzzle Wisps and Muzzle Flares, and does not ignite Coalers. The Tear Jerker also puts out decorative torches and burning skeletons. This gun and both of its synergies are references to The Binding of Isaac.

Smiley's Revolver is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Decreases shop prices by 15% while in the players inventory. Notes: This item's description references both the name and effect of Disarming Personality. The entry for Smiley's Revolver in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of Smiley.

Megahand is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires small yellow pellets that deal 6 damage. Charges up to fire a powerful blue energy blast that deals 45 damage and knocks enemies back. Notes: This gun is a reference to Mega Man's arm cannon, called the Mega Buster. The tooltip references how in Mega Man through Mega Man 3, the Mega Buster was represented in menus by the Symbol P. The colors the Megahand flashes while charging most closely resemble those in Mega Man 4, the game in which the ability to charge the Buster was first introduced.

Serious Cannon is a gun that fires large, bouncing cannonballs that pierce through enemies. The cannonballs can bounce up to 6 times before disappearing. Each shot can hit the same enemy multiple times. Notes: This gun is a reference to the SBC Cannon from Serious Sam. Its item name "Seriously MENTAL" is a reference to Mental, the series' main villain. The synergy Serious Sam Publishing Contract is a reference to Serious Sam: Kamikaze Attack!, another game published by Devolver Digital.

Magnum is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon, as well as in Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead under a different name. Notes: The Magnum is the most commonly used enemy weapon in the Gungeon, being held by Bullet Kin, Veteran Bullet Kin, Minelets, Cardinals, Shroomers, Ashen Bullet Kin, Mutant Bullet Kin... The quote "5 shots or 6?" is a reference to the film Dirty Harry. The gun's description also is a reference to Harry calling his magnum a filthy gun.

RPG is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a rocket-propelled explosive grenade. Capable of dealing considerable damage, but with a lengthy reload time. The impact of the RPG projectile (not the explosion) deals 30% extra damage to bosses. RPG grenades start off slightly slower, before quickly accelerating to maximum velocity. Notes: If the player feeds both this gun and Bee Hive to a Muncher, it will spit out a Stinger as a result. 'Gundropping' (dropping a weapon mid-reload and picking it back up to force-reload it, skipping it's reload time) is highly effective with this weapon due to it's lengthy reload.

Freeze Ray is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires a continuous blue laser that freezes enemies. Notes: Has a similar look and basic abilities as the weapon used by Mr. Freeze in various "Batman" media. This gun's flavor text is a reference to Arnold Schwarzenegger's campy performance as Mr. Freeze from the movie Batman & Robin. The synergy "Willing to Sacrifice" is a reference to the 1977 song Cold As Ice by Foreigner.

Heroine is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It has 3 levels of damage depending on how long the gun has been charged. Notes: The Heroine is a reference to the Arm Cannon wielded by Samus Aran, the heavily armored bounty hunter and protagonist in the Metroid series. Its in-game model and its alternative modes match its appearance in Metroid Prime. The Hyper Beam synergy is a reference to a weapon with the same name from Super Metroid. The beam is obtained after a baby Metroid sacrifices itself to save Samus.

Trank Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: This gun is wielded by Beadies, Coalers, and the Beholster. As it is held by the Beholster, it can be placed on the Beholster Shrine. The Trank Gun is a reference to the Tranq Gun from the Metal Gear franchise, in particular a modified Beretta M9 used in Metal Gear Solid: The Twin Snakes and Metal Gear Solid 2...

Machine Pistol is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: This gun resembles a Mini Uzi, a compact, blowback-operated machine pistol developed in 1980, chambered in 9x19mm Parabellum. This gun is used by Bandana Bullet Kin, and is one of the six guns wielded by the Beholster. As it is held by the Beholster, it can be placed on the Beholster Shrine.

Skull Spitter is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: ETG Fires piercing, homing (Rad: 6 Vel: 500) skulls. Notes: This gun appears similar to Johnson, the talking gun from the 2011 video game Shadows of the Damned. The description of the gun is a reference to the Jake Clover game, Nuign Spectre. Dawn of the Gundead is a reference to the emblematic zombie film Dawn of the Dead.

Jolter is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. It fires large, rectangular bullets with high knockback. Notes: The Jolter is a reference to the Warhammer 40k weapon, the Bolter, typically used by space marines. However, unlike the Bolter, the Jolter isn't an explosive weapon. Having the Demon Head orbital while using the Gun That Can Kill The Past may crash the game and force you to restart the floor. The Synergy "There is Only War" is a reference to Warhammer 40k's iconic tagline: "In the grim darkness of the far future, there is only war."

Sniper Rifle is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires high-velocity bullets that can pierce through one enemy. Notes: The synergy 360 Yes Scope is a reference to the 360 No Scope meme spawned by Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare. The Diazepam and Sniper Woof synergies are referencing Metal Gear Solid's Sniper Wolf character. "Scope Creep" is a reference to a common problem in many projects where the original goals expand while the project is in progress.

SAA is a gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. In the Apple Arcade version of Exit the Gungeon, the SAA is called the Single Action. Effects: The SAA fires bouncing bullets that can ricochet up to two times. This damage multiplier stacks each time the bullet ricochets. Additional bounces above the guns inherent 2 can be granted by items such as Bouncy Bullets. In addition to this, the SAA is notable for its very rapid reload. Notes: SAA refers to the Colt Single Action Army. If combined with Angry Bullets, the shots fired from the SAA will often bounce around the room multiple times, great for fighting large, slow moving enemies.

Regular Shotgun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon, Exit the Gungeon, and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. Notes: The Regular Shotgun has an equip animation in which the gungeoneer racks the pump. ETG The Regular Shotgun is in the SHOTGUN gun class, meaning it benefits from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. The Regular Shotgun is the gun wielded by Red Shotgun Kin, Ashen Shotgun Kin, and Mutant Shotgun Kin.

Crescent Crossbow is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires medium-sized star projectiles. These stars split into 3 smaller, bouncing stars upon impact. These extra stars are created at the end of the medium star's small trail, causing them to appear slightly away from the wall, enemy, or obstacle the initial star impacted. When fully charged, fires a large star. This star splits into 10 medium-size stars, which in turn each split into 3 smaller stars. Notes: The Crescent Crossbow is peculiarly a part of the CHARM gun class despite not charming enemies. This gun is a reference to the Crescent Moon Wand and Moon Scepter weapons in the Sailor Moon franchise, which use moonlight to combat evil.

AU Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. In both games, it deals very high damage but has very little ammo. Notes: It is worth noting that due to the DPS cap mechanic existent in most Bosses, shots from the AU Gun should ideally be fired 3 seconds apart. This gun is a reference to the Golden Gun from the James Bond movie The Man with the Golden Gun. "Some Assembly Required" refers to the fact that in the film, the Golden Gun is assembled from a series of golden innocuous-looking items: a fountain pen (the barrel), a cigarette...

Laser Rifle is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It fires a burst of three instantaneous purple laser bolts. Notes: The weapon is likely a reference to the Laser Rifle from X-COM: UFO Defense, which it strongly resembles in both appearance and function. It may also be a reference to the more recent X-COM games, which also sport similar looking laser weapons. If the player also has Vorpal Bullets while they have the Starburst Fire synergy, it is possible for Vorpal Bullets to trigger multiple times in a row, and will deal massive damag...

Void Shotgun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon, Exit the Gungeon, and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. It fires a shotgun blast of 6ETGXTG/4HoTG short-range purple bullets and has strong knockback. Notes: The Void Shotgun is in the SHOTGUN gun class, and as such is affected by the Shotgun Affinity synergy. CQC stands for Close Quarters Combat. This gun is a reference to the Junker Legionnaire Shotgun from the tabletop miniatures game, Void 1.1.

38 Special is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: Spamming the fire button while dodge rolling causes the gun to fire considerably faster for a brief time after the dodge roll has concluded. With a fire rate of 0.07, the 38 Special requires a minimum of ~14 clicks per second to fire at maximum DPS, meaning the player is likely to be unable to take full advantage of th... This can be mitigated on PC by temporarily binding the player's fire keys to ScrollUp and ScrollDown on the scroll wheel.

Alien Sidearm is a gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Its behaviour is starkly different in each game. Notes: This gun is a reference to the alien pistols usable in Halo. Shield Breaker is a reference to Halo's famous shield mechanic. The charging mechanic is also a function of Halo's plasma pistol. A fully charged shot from that gun could instantly break another player's shields in multiplayer.

Void Core Assault Rifle is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. Effects: ETG Fires a 3-shot burst of lasers. HoTG Rapid-fires laser blasts. Notes: Prior to release, this gun was originally known as the Space Core Assault Rifle with 250 ammo and 30 magazine size. The name of the synergy "Armored Corps" is a homophone to the Armored Core game series. The Void Core Captain described in this gun's Ammonomicon entry is Alistair, the Thunderbolt, referenced in the Ammonomicon entries of many electricity-themed guns and items.

Hegemony Rifle is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires blue energy bolts with considerable spread. Notes: This gun appears to be based on the DH-17 Carbine from Star Wars. The comment about accuracy likely references the historically bad aim of Stormtroopers in Star Wars. However, Stormtroopers never used the DH-17. The weapon's sprites refer to it as "E12", which is a reference to the E-11 Carbine which is the actual standard-issue weapon of Stormtroopers.

Demon Head is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires a continuous beam that slightly curves towards enemies and has a chance to burn. Notes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Demon Head did not curve towards enemies. This gun is a reference to the Guardian Head weapon from Shadow Warrior. The synergy There is Only War is a reference to the tagline for Warhammer 40,000 by Games Workshop.

Bundle of Wands is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a spread of three magic projectiles that each have a 10% chance to transmogrify enemies they hit into harmless chickens. Bosses and Mimics are immune to being transmogrified. Transmogrification can affect enemies which are normally invulnerable, such as Gunreapers and Lead Cubes. Notes: Despite being a shotgun, the Bundle of Wands is not in the SHOTGUN gun class, and as such does not benefit from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. Shooting walls with the gun leaves rune-shaped indentations. The Special Delivery synergy is a reference to Hedwig, the snowy owl that accompanies Harry in the Harry Potter book series.

Colt 1851 is a gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon that fires a burst of two bullets. In the Apple Arcade version of Exit the Gungeon, the Colt 1851 is called Blondie. Notes: The description and pickup quote are referencing the gun's use in the film, The Good, the Bad, and the Ugly. The name 'Blondie' used for the Colt 1851 in the Apple Arcade version of Exit the Gungeon is a reference to the nickname given to the Man With No Name in the film by the character... This gun is used in The Hunter's past.

Medkit is an item that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. In ETG it appears as an active item, while in XTG it appears as a passive item. Effects: ETG Heals the player for four hearts on use. XTG Heals the player for four hearts immediately when collected. Has no additional effect. Notes: Has no effect on The Robot. If the Vampire is present on the floor, buying this item from the shop and selling the resulting health is almost always a profit. The description refers to the NGO "Médecin Sans Frontières" (doctors without borders) but parodied to be "Médecin Sans Diplôme" (doctors without diploma)

Potion of Lead Skin is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: While active, the player turns gray, becoming invincible and reflecting bullets back at enemies. Reflected bullets deal 10 damage. Does not prevent damage from falling into pits. Notes: This item is a reference to the classic Potion of Stoneskin from Dungeons and Dragons. Both the tagline and the item's effect could be references to Metal Gear Rising's Sundowner, a boss who employs a shield to deflect the player's attacks while boasting of his invi... The tagline could also be a reference to the James Bond movie Goldeneye, in which computer technician Boris Grishenko repeatedly yells, "Yes! I am invincible!"

Knife Shield is an active item in Enter the Gungeon Effects: Creates a ring of 8 knives around the player that damage enemies and block enemy bullets. Knives vanish after taking enough damage. If used again before Knife Shield is recharged, the knives are launched forward. Each knife deals 30 damage. Notes: The knives do not carry on between floors, and disappear once the player enters a new floor. Knife Shield can be activated while the player already has knives, adding another 8 knives to the ring. Knives will destroy some objects, such as breakables and barrels.

Proximity Mine is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Places a mine that explodes when an enemy comes near it, dealing 60 damage. Notes: The mine does not seem to explode upon contact with mimics, even when already revealed. This item is a reference to the Proximity Mine in GoldenEye 007.

Bullet Time is an active item. Effects: Slows down time by 70% while active. The player's movement, firing and reload speeds are unaffected by the slowdown. Notes: Bluer Guon Stone - If the player also has Blue Guon Stone, the size and rotation speed of the Guon Stone is increased and it rotates at a fixed distance from the player while they... Thrown For A Bloop - If the player also has Blooper, while Bullet Time is active, a Blooper orbits the player and continually fires at enemies. Trivia: The tagline "Dodge This" is a reference to a quote made by the character Trinity in The Matrix.

Decoy is an active item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Places a decoy at the users current position. This decoy will distract enemies, who will target the decoy instead of the player. If a decoy is placed in a Shop, it will also distract the shopkeeper, allowing the player to steal from that shop while the Decoy is inside. Notes: Items cannot be purchased from a shop with a decoy in it; stealing is the only option. The item itself is a reference to the Body Replacement Technique from Naruto, in which the user replaces themselves with a log to avoid an opponents attack. The Kage Bunshin synergy refers to the Kage Bunshin technique from Naruto.

Bubble Shield is an unused passive item. Effects: Deflects bullets for a few seconds, similar to Potion of Lead Skin. Recharges once every two rooms cleared. Trivia: It is unknown why this item was cut.

Supply Drop is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. The Marine starts with this item. Effects: Upon use, calls in a crate containing Ammo at the players position. Cannot be used if the player is holding a gun with full ammo. Notes: Supply Drops have a very low chance to drop on room clear. Players can stack multiple uses if they acquire more than one Supply Drop. Calling in an ammo drop on the Sell Creeps grate allows the player to sell the resulting pickup.

Makarov is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: This gun is based on the real life Russian firearm Pistolet Makarova (Makarov's Pistol). This gun's description references The Cosmonaut, who was originally going to be found in the game as a Gungeoneer, but got cut during development. "This gun is no better or worse than any other gun." is a reference to equality under the doctrine of Communism, which was prevalent during the creation of the Makarov.

The Budget Revolver is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It serves as The Convict's starter gun in both games. Notes: Cannot be dropped. Cannot be obtained by any Gungeoneer other than The Convict or The Paradox. The gun appears to be modeled after a variety of snub-nosed revolvers.

Deck4rd is a gun that fires high-damage exploding bullets. Notes: Although classified as semi-automatic, the Deck4rd is functionally automatic, as its rate of fire does not benefit from tapping the fire button instead of holding it down. Blade Runner - If the player has Origuni, magazine size of both guns is increased by 50%. Running Blades - If the player has Knife Shield, killing enemies with Deck4rd has a high chance to spawn Knife Shield knives. Trivia: This gun is a reference to Deckard's gun from Blade Runner. "Unicorn of Handguns" is a reference to the unicorn in Deckard's dreams.

Elephant Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: ETG Fires a conical spread of bullets with strong knockback. Notes: So called elephant guns were large guns designed for the hunting of large animals (such as elephants) during safaris in Africa in the 19th century. The Ammonomicon description "These guns were once used to make pianos out of teeth." is a reference to the practice of hunting elephants to harvest their tusks for ivory, a materi... The Ammonomicon description "An inelegant weapon for a less civilized age" is a reference to the iconic line "This is the weapon of a Jedi Knight.

Unfinished Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires piercing projectiles. Notes: Despite not actually igniting enemies, the Unfinished Gun is in the FIRE gun class. As a consequence, having the Unfinished Gun in the player's inventory makes them less likely to find other guns in the FIRE class that actually burn enemies. The Blacksmith can be seen working on an Unfinished Gun.

The Vulcan Cannon is a gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. In the Apple Arcade version of Exit the Gungeon, the Vulcan Cannon is called the Gull Cannon. Notes: According to its ammonomicon entry, the Vulcan Cannon is wielded by the Gatling Gull, however its visual appearance is starkly different to the minigun the Gatling Gull can be see... The gun is a reference to a common trope of action films where people will carry handheld M134 Miniguns, which in reality is very difficult and impractical. The Not Quite As Mini synergy is a reference to the Minigun from Team Fortress 2 in appereance and function and subsequently reaffirms this.

The Marine Sidearm is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It serves as The Marine's starting gun in both games. It has a good accuracy and a stronger knockback than other starting guns. Notes: Cannot be dropped. Is technically automatic, as tapping the fire button does not provide a faster fire rate than holding it down. After defeating The Marine's past with his alternate skin, an alternate skin of this weapon is unlocked.

Gamma Ray is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a green laser beam that slightly curves towards enemies. Continually shooting an enemy with the laser for 0.25 seconds will poison them for 3.5 seconds. Bug This beam is capable of extinguishing the fireplace in the Keep of The Lead Lord, despite not leaving any goop in its base form. Notes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Gamma Ray did not curve towards enemies. The Ammonomicon quote "Mean Green" may be a reference to The Hulk, who was transformed by exposure to gamma radiation. The Beta Ray synergy is likely a reference to beta decay particles.

Robot's Right Hand is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It serves as The Robot's starter weapon in both games. Notes: Cannot be dropped. Cannot be obtained by any Gungeoneer other than The Robot or The Paradox. Robot's Right Hand has the largest magazine size of the starting guns.

The Rogue Special is a Gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It is the starting weapon of The Pilot. Notes: Cannot be dropped. Cannot be obtained by any Gungeoneer other than The Pilot or The Paradox. While reloading, the gun emits three jets of fog in the shape of a radiation symbol.

Eye of the Beholster is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires instant laser projectiles. The final shot of each cylinder fires a projectile shaped like a pink Beadie. Notes: The Beholster wields this gun. As it is held by the Beholster, it can be placed on the Beholster Shrine. The friendly Beadie spawned at the end of each cylinder is actually a bullet, not a charmed enemy. This is why the player cannot kill it and why it only travels in a straight line.

H4mmer is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. Effects: Rapid-fires a barrage of bullets. The last shot in every magazine is a high-damage hammer. ETG This hammer has a 20% chance to stun enemies for 3.5 seconds. Notes: This gun may be a reference to the trick of "fanning the hammer", where a single-action revolver is fired by flicking the hammer of the weapon rather than pulling the trigger in o... Bug There is an issue with the Hammer and Nail synergy where if the ammo count exceeds what the base gun would have without the 50% maximum ammo, the current ammo count would reve...

Stinger is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires an explosive missile, and three homing (Rad: 10 Vel: 150) bees that seek out and attack enemies. When the missile impacts an enemy or obstacle, it will create 5 additional bees. Notes: Giving the Bee Hive and RPG to the Muncher will always result in a Stinger. The Stingers long reload time makes it especially affected by the 'gun-dropping' technique. This gun is based on the real world FIM-92 Stinger, a United States military ground-to-air missile launcher. Its ability to fire bees is a double-meaning based on its name.

Old Goldie is a shotgun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a tight spread of shotgun pellets that deal significant damage, but have short range. Increases Coolness by 1. Increases damage by 70% while held. Notes: When a gun is Duct Taped to Old Goldie, it too will receive the 70% damage boost. However, when Old Goldie is duct taped to another gun, the damage boost is lost. Old Goldie is in the SHOTGUN gun class, meaning it benefits from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. This weapon's name is likely a play on "Golden Oldie", a nostalgic term used to refer fondly to old music or movies.

MAC10 is a gun in Enter the Gungeon that fires bursts of 4 bullets. Notes: The item's subtext is a reference to the Biggie Smalls song 'Come On'. The synergy with the Eyepatch is a reference to Kurt Russell as Snake Plissken in the 1981 movie Escape from New York. The MAC10 is based on the real life machine pistol of the same name.

AKEY-47 is a gun in Enter the Gungeon that fires keys. Shooting a locked chest or door will unlock it. Alternatively, opening a chest or door with the AKEY-47 equipped will unlock it for free, if the player has no keys. Notes: The AKEY-47 Has identical statistics to the AK-47. Once unlocked, the AKEY-47 may appear for sale in Flynts shop for 3 keys. Its bullets cannot open the padlocks on the trapdoor to the Oubliette and the tunnel to the Resourceful Rat's Lair; however, they can be opened by hand for free with the AKEY-47 e...

M16 is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. In the Apple Arcade version of Exit the Gungeon, the M16 is called the iCarbine. Effects: Reloading the M16 alternates between a burst-fire mode which shoots a burst of three bullets with every trigger pull, and a grenade launcher mode which lobs explosive projectiles. XTG Grenades are affected by gravity, falling quickly towards the bottom of the screen. Notes: Grenades consume 1 ammo each, the exact same as a regular bullet. Allowing the M16 to reload passively by switching to another weapon for a few seconds does not switch the gun's form. An old, unused appearance for the M16 exists within Enter the Gungeons assets.

Polaris is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Has three different levels, with the gun leveling up after enough enemies are slain. Higher levels deal more damage. Killing 11 enemies will level up Polaris to level 2. Killing 20 more enemies (31 total) will level up Polaris to level 3. Notes: Polaris' level does not affect its selling price. While level 1 and 3 fire a single projectile of varying damage, level 2 fires two. Losing hearts in ways that do not deal damage to the player, such as the Vampire, do not affect the level of Polaris.

Patriot is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires high-accuracy bullets that can pierce up to 3 times. Rate of fire increases the longer the trigger is held. Notes: This gun is a reference to the weapon of the same name in Metal Gear Solid 3: Snake Eater. The Ammonomicon quote "Gun Eater" is a reference to the title of the game- "Snake Eater". This gun's description is a reference to the convoluted and hard to follow plot of the Metal Gear series, and how people still enjoy it regardless.

The Rusty Sidearm is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It serves as The Hunters starting gun in both games. Notes: Cannot be dropped. Cannot be obtained by any Gungeoneer other than The Hunter or The Paradox. After defeating The Hunter's past with her alternate skin, an alternate skin for this gun is unlocked.

Unicorn Horn is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It fires a continuous rainbow beam with strong knockback that homes in on enemies. A unique song is played whenever it is fired. Notes: The song played by the Unicorn Horn is Unicorn Power!, composed by Enter the Gungeon sound designer Erica Hampson. If the player has Helix Bullets, the 2 rainbows fired will follow the same path, and will not lose their homing property, unlike most other homing guns with Helix Bullets. The rainbow beam and unique music track of this weapon may be a reference to the Parry Gripp song Space Unicorn.

Scope is a passive item. Effects: Reduces shot spread by 60%, increasing accuracy. Notes: 360 Yes Scope - While holding A.W.P. or Sniper Rifle, spinning 360 degrees before firing makes the shot deal 50% more damage. The buff can stack up to 6 times, and each instance of the buff will last for 3 seconds, providing an additional 1.5x damage multiplier for each instance. If player has several Scopes or Lich's Eye Bullets, the buff can stack 1.5x damage multiplier for each item. Trivia: The synergy M1 Multi-Tool refers to the MIDA Multi Tool of Destiny, which grants increased movement speed and boasts a very high reload.

Ration is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Heals the player for two full hearts. Automatically used if the player runs out of health. If the player has more than one active item, the Ration has to be the selected active item to be automatically used upon death. Notes: Has no effect for The Robot. Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Ration was an tier item. This is a direct reference to the rations from Metal Gear, both in terms of its description and function, as well as the auto-heal property.

Fortune's Favor is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: While active, enemy bullets are repulsed from the player- curving around them. Notes: The curving effect is not powerful enough to redirect bullets with significant velocity (e.g. Sniper Shell projectiles) or bigger bullets (e.g. Cannonbalrog's disappearing attack). The synergy "Pretty Good" is a reference to the popular quote from Metal Gear Solid, "You're pretty good". The synergy "What a Thrill" is a reference to the song "Snake Eater" from Metal Gear Solid 3, sung by Cynthia Harrel.

Jetpack is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Use to toggle the jetpack on and off. While active, the players Movement Speed is increased by 2.5 and the player gains flight, but cannot dodge roll. Passing over pools of flammable goop such as oil or Phaser Spider webs while the jetpack is active will set them on fire. Notes: The Barrage Shot synergy is a reference to the game Charge Shot, which the weapon of the same name in Enter the Gungeon is a reference to. Bug If Jetpack is active when entering the Resourceful Rat's Lair, the loading screen will be the default loading screen instead of the rat variant.

Raiden Coil is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires a continuous laser that homes in on and damages all on-screen enemies. Notes: Like other guns, this will not fire if the player is up against a wall and trying to shoot into the wall, even though this gun shoots through walls. Backup Gun indirectly doubles this gun's damage due to its direct homing. This gun is a large reference to the Raiden series.

Bomb is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. It also appears in Exit the Gungeon as a powerup. Effects: ETG Throws a bomb onto the ground that explodes after a short delay, dealing 60 damage to nearby enemies. XTG Triggers an explosion at the position of every enemy on screen. Notes: The bomb's explosion can open secret rooms. Bomb can be used to hurt Cannonbalrog while it is performing its invulnerable attack. The Bomb's synergy with the Bow is likely a reference to the Legend of Zelda series.

Ice Bomb is an active item. Effects: Throws a bomb that deals 8 damage and freezes enemies. Notes: Smart Bombs - If the player has Homing Bullets or Crutch, Ice Bomb will home in on enemies. Battle Mode - If the player has Orbital Bullets, creates three bombs which orbit around the player, blocking bullets and exploding in contact with enemies. Super Bomberpal - If the player has Lil' Bomber, it fires two bombs instead of one, charges twice as fast, and has 20% lower reload time.

Magic Sweet is a passive item. Effects: Adds 1 heart container. Increases movement speed by 1.5. Increases damage by 15%. Notes: Sweetness and Light - If the player also has Magic Lamp, it fires three projectiles at a time. The shots deal 25% of their original damage, but the genie deals full damage. Like other items that grant heart containers, this item gives The Robot 7 to 10 upon pickup. Trivia: This item is a reference to the item Rare Candy from the Pokémon series, which levels up the Pokémon by 1, explaining the all stats up. The Sweetness and Light synergy is a reference to an English idiom.

Heavy Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade. Effects: Increases damage by 25%. Halves bullet speed. Doubles knockback. Notes: Thrown guns will increase in size, and will remain large even after picking them back up. This is purely a cosmetic effect and has no effect on gameplay. Due to the higher knockback and slower shot speed, this item can be detrimental with short-ranged guns. Rocket-Powered Bullets will mitigate the lower bullet velocity. Trivia: This item is a reference to the game Heavy Bullets. The icon of the item resembles the appearance of ammunition in Heavy Bullets. The statement about their colors is a reference to the colorful graphics in the aforementioned game.

Cartographer's Ring is a passive item. Effects: New floors have a 50% chance to be mapped out. Does not reveal secret rooms. Notes: Cartographer's Ring does not reveal the trapdoor to the Resourceful Rat's Lair.

Rocket-Powered Bullets is a passive item and bullet upgrade that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Increases the shot speed of the player's bullets by 50%. Increases damage by 10%. Increases rate of fire by 10%. Notes: The active item exclusive to The Pilot's past that fires a volley of rockets shares an identical sprite with Rocket-Powered Bullets.

Bionic Leg is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Increases Movement Speed by 1.5. Grants a piece of Armor. Notes: The quote upon picking up this item is a reference to a Star Wars: Episode VI – Return of the Jedi quote. The synergy Brave New World is a reference to the Disney movie, "The Little Mermaid". The synergy is also a reference to the novel Brave New World by Aldous Huxley

Ballot is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Increases Coolness by 3, which decreases the cooldown of active items and increases the chance of items dropping upon clearing a room. Notes: If duplicates of this item are somehow obtained, further increases Coolness. This item being in a gun-themed game is likely a reference to the Abraham Lincoln quote "The ballot is stronger than the bullet." It may also simply be due to the words ballot and bullet being near homophones.

Ammo Synthesizer is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Adds a 10% chance to regain 5% of the currently held gun's maximum ammo (rounded down) upon killing an enemy. Notes: If 5% of a gun's maximum ammo is less than 1 (less than 20 maximum ammo), Ammo Synthesizer will restore 1 ammo when it activates. Works well with Ancient Hero's Bandana as it will restore 4x more ammo effectively making it easier to not run out. Works well with Makeshift Cannon if it activates.

Eyepatch is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Increases damage by 35%. Increases shot spread by 65%, making shots less accurate. As this serves as a multiplier on a gun's existing shot spread, guns that are more accurate by default will be less affected. Notes: This item is a reference to the Norse god Odin, in particular the poem Völuspá of the Poetic Edda, where it is explained that Odin cast his eye into the well 'Mímisbrunnr' in exch... The Iroquois synergy is a reference to the 1981 film Escape from New York, where the protagonist Snake Plissken wears an eyepatch over his left eye and carries a MAC10.

Metronome is a passive item. Effects: Grants a +2% damage bonus for each enemy killed. The effect maxes out at +150% weapon damage (75 consecutive kills). The bonus is reset if the player takes damage, changes guns, or drops their gun. Picking up a new gun does not reset the bonus, effectively transferring the bonus to the new gun. Notes: Adding currently equipped guns to a Muncher will reset the bonus as if switching guns. Playing Winchester's minigame will reset the bonus. Receiving and losing the Windgunner from Sprun will not reset the bonus.

Disintegrator is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires a powerful disintegrating laser after a long charge time. Notes: The Disintegrator appears to be a reference to the "M-920 Cain" heavy weapon from Mass Effect 2, having a similar design and very long charge time before firing.

Blunderbuss is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It charges up to fire a short ranged shotgun blast of bouncing bullets. Notes: Despite firing like a shotgun, the Blunderbuss is not in the SHOTGUN gun class, and as such does not benefit from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. There is a glitch where firing Blunderbuss by charging until just before the fully charged animation causes the gun to fire without using ammo. The description in the Ammonomicon is a reference to Farmer Giles of Ham by J.R.R. Tolkien.

Pulse Cannon is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires a spread of 6 projectiles. Notes: This weapon may be a reference to the M41A Pulse Rifle in the film Aliens. The entry for the Pulse Cannon in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of a monstrous Bullet Kin with another Bullet Kin head emerging out of its toothy maw.

Cactus is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It rapidly fires low-damage needles. Notes: "1000 Needles!" is a reference to a move used by the Cactuar, a recurring enemy from the Final Fantasy series, who resembles a walking cactus. "Succulent DOOM" may possibly be a reference to the album Special Herbs by MF DOOM. The "undiscovered Chamber" in the Cactus' description may be the unused Old West floor.

Flame Hand is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Notes: As the Flame Hand deals fire-type damage, it deals no damage to Muzzle Wisps and Muzzle Flares. ETG Due to the high knockback and large size of the fireballs, they can hit the same enemy multiple times. 2D6 + Int mod is a reference to Dungeons and Dragons

Shotbow is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires shotgun spreads of 6 crossbow bolts at once. Notes: The Shotbow is in the SHOTGUN gun class and as such benefits from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. This gun's description could be a reference to the episode Tuvix, from the TV show Star Trek Voyager, where two characters were merged into one as a result of a transporter (a sor... It could also be a reference to the short story by George Langelaan titled "The Fly" originally published in the June 1957 issue of Playboy magazine, which inspired two films of t...

Junk is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. It often appears after destroying chests. Effects: Junk has no inherent effect, however it does interact with some other systems and items. When playing as The Robot, each piece of Junk increases damage by a permanent additive 5%. The damage increase persists no matter what happens to the Junk. If the player has Ser Junkan, collecting Junk levels him up, making him more powerful. Notes: Junk has a chest destruction loot weight of 0.45. If the player is in possession of Ser Junkan, the chest destruction loot weight of junk is multiplied by 1.5. If the player is in possession of at least one key, the chest destruction loot weight of junk is multiplied by 3.

RUBE-ADYNE MK.II is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires purple lasers that can each bounce up to 2 times. Notes: RUBE-ADYNE MK.II is the upgraded version of the RUBE-ADYNE Prototype. The RUBE-ABYNE MK.II multiplies the loot weight of the RUBE-ADYNE Prototype by 2, doubling its likelihood of appearing. Curiously, this does not work in reverse. Obtaining the RUBE-ADYNE Prototype does not make the RUBE-ADYNE MK.II more likely to appear.

Com4nd0 is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires explosive homing (Rad: Vel: 220) missiles. Notes: This gun is one of the six wielded by the Beholster. When used by the Beholster, it fires homing rockets visually identical to those fired by the player. As it is held by the Beholster, it can be placed on the Beholster Shrine. This gun is based on the real world M202 FLASH rocket launcher.

Glacier is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires bouncing ice cubes which leave trails of water, explode, and freeze enemies. Like most explosions, these ice blasts can destroy enemy bullets. Notes: The ice cubes bounce off Cursed Pots and will destroy them without exploding. Prior to the Advanced Gungeons & Draguns Update, Glacier was a gun. The Unsinkable synergy is a reference to the RMS Titanic, an ocean liner that was claimed to be 'unsinkable', which famously sank after a collision with an iceberg.

Utility Belt is a passive item. Effects: Grants an extra active item slot. Increases maximum ammo by 20%. Notes: Gunnerang - If the player also has Boomerang, thrown guns deal 50% more damage. M1 Multi-Tool - If the player also has M1, M1's reload time is decreased by 20%, and its magazine size and maximum ammo are increased. If duplicates of this item are somehow obtained, increases the maximum ammo count and grants another active item slot. Trivia: Unused: Wonderful Toys - (CustomSynergyType.USEFUL_TOOLS) If the player has Escape Rope, it causes an unknown effect. This item and its synergy with the Boomerang are likely a reference to Batman's utility belt and Batarangs, and possibly his aversion to guns.

Ring of Miserly Protection is a passive item. Effects: Grants two heart containers. Disappears upon purchasing anything from any shop. Notes: Porkulent - If the player has Pig, purchases will not destroy the ring. The item disappears if it is dropped. The ring cannot be sold, as it disappears immediately even if dropped atop the Sell Creep's grate. Trivia: The Ring of Miserly Protection is a reference to a ring in Dark Souls called the "Ring of Favor and Protection", which boosts several stats but breaks when removed.

Backpack is a passive item. Effects: Allows the player to hold an extra active item. The active items are switched by default by pressing Shift (keyboard) or up on the D-pad (controller). Notes: M1 Multi-Tool - If the player also has M1, its reload time is decreased by 20%, and its magazine size and maximum ammo are increased. Mak Pak - If the player has Makarov, Makarov's maximum ammo is increased by 30%. While holding Marakov, it occasionally fires a homing bullet at an enemy for free. MM6 Mini Rocket - If the player also has Jetpack, activating the Jetpack fires a rocket in the direction of the crosshair. This has a cooldown of about 5 seconds.

Ammo Belt is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Increases maximum ammo by 20%. Notes: The M1 Multi-Tool synergy is a possible reference to the MIDA Multi-Tool, a gun from the game Destiny. An Ammo Belt can be seen among the pile of items that makes up the Resourceful Rats throne in the Resourceful Rat's Lair. Bug Entering a Gungeoneer's past with Ammo Belt in your inventory will cause the gun given in that Gungeoneer's past to have its current ammo set to 1.2x of the gun's usual maximu...

Cog of Battle is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: When reloading, pressing the fire or reload buttons when the indicator passes a certain mark on the reload bar will instantly reload the weapon. This effectively decreases the reload time. This effect also increases the damage of the next magazine by 75% and also increases the accuracy of the next magazine. Notes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Cog of Battle was a item. All active reload effects (including those of the Cog of Battle) are disabled while the Gun Queue challenge modifier is active. The Cog of Battle's name and active reload mechanic are a reference to the Gears of War series.

C4 is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Places explosives on the ground which then can be detonated by using the item again, dealing 60 damage to nearby enemies. Unlike Bomb, does not open secret rooms. Notes: The C4 in game is based on the real-life plastic explosive. C4 is often known for its moldable clay-like quality; however, this doesn't appear in-game. After placing down the C4 charge, the items appearance in the players UI will change to a transmitter switch until the mine is detonated. Dropping C4 after placing down a mine, but before triggering it will automatically detonate the charge, reverting the item back to its regular appearance.

Map is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon, which is treated more like a pickup. Effects: Fully reveals the layout of the current floor, including secret rooms. Notes: Map is consumed upon pickup and does not appear in the player's inventory. Has a small chance to drop on room clear. Does not reveal the trapdoor to the Resourceful Rat's Lair.

Honeycomb is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Taking damage spawns 12-20 homing (Rad: 10 Vel: 150) bees that seek out and attack enemies. These bees are not affected by the player's other items, or any of their stats except for bullet scale. Notes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Honeycomb was a item. This item is a reference to The Pain, a character in Metal Gear Solid 3: Snake Eater capable of controlling insects due to a parasite infection giving him the ability to secrete p... It may also be a reference to the Honey Comb item in Terraria, which has an identical effect.

Master of Unlocking is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Has a 20% chance to give a free Key upon clearing a room if no damage was taken in that room. Notes: In practice, this item can almost fully substitute for infinite unlock items due to the sheer amount of keys it generates (assuming the player has little to no Curse). This item is a reference to the character Jill Valentine from Resident Evil and her ability to pick locks. The item's name is a reference to an infamous voice line in the first Resident Evil game: "Jill, here’s a lockpick.

The RUBE-ADYNE Prototype is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It fires lasers which bounce up to 6 times. Notes: The RUBE-ADYNE MK.II is an upgraded version of this gun. The RUBE-ADYNE weapons may be named after David Rubel, one of the programmers of Enter the Gungeon. As Rubel is one of the lead programmers at Dodge Roll, the Ammonomicon entry of this gun may be a reference to the development of Enter the Gungeon itself, with Rubel needing to p...

Shotgun Full of Hate is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a spread of 6 disparate bullets with different effects. 2 regular bullets with lower range than the other projectiles. 2 poison bullets that create pools of poison goop on impact. As poison is a non-flammable liquid, these bullets are capable of extinguishing fused chests and the fireplace. Notes: The Shotgun Full of Hate being in the SHOTGUN gun class makes it inherently less common than its counterpart, the Shotgun Full of Love which exists in the CHARM class. This is due to the SHOTGUN class being a more common class, meaning more guns exist where possessing them will make the Shotgun Full of Hate less common.

Witch Pistol is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Each bullet fired has a 10% chance to transmogrify enemies into harmless chickens. Notes: Bosses and Mimics are immune to transmogrification. Bullets from the Witch Pistol leave R, N, and Φ shaped scorch marks on impacting a north wall. These are the same symbols created and fired by Bookllets.

Dragunfire is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires piercing flame bullets that have a 1/9 chance to burn enemies. Notes: The design of the Dragunfire is that of the Bolter from the dystopian science-fantasy universe of Warhammer 40,000. The name of the gun comes from the Dragonfire round which the Bolter can fire. The tagline "Roar" is a reference to the Bolter's unique roaring sound when fired.

Lies is a passive item. Effects: None. Notes: Betrayer's Lies - If the player has Betrayer's Shield, it gains infinite ammo, a much larger magazine size, increased accuracy, and extremely high fire rate. Wicked Sister - If the player also has Magnum, Magnum gets 200 max ammo and a knife on the front of the gun that will block bullets when reloading. The only way to obtain this item is by destroying Brother Albern's chest.

Face Melter is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Simultaneously fires four streams of musical notes at 90° intervals. Creates an amplifier on the ground when reloading. While firing, the active amplifier will fire bullets of its own in sync with the main gun, including aim angle. Only one amplifier can be active at a time. Creating a new amplifier by reloading will remove any previous ones. Notes: Amplifiers do not have hitboxes, meaning that enemies can potentially stand on top of them and be damaged by all four note streams at once. Possible Bug In co-op, if playing as The Cultist, reloading will drop the amplifier at the other player's location.

T-Shirt Cannon is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires t-shirts with considerable knockback. Notes: This weapon and its high knockback are a reference to an episode of The Simpsons, in which Maude Flanders is shot by a T-Shirt Cannon and falls to her death. The T-Shirt Cannon was first teased in a twitter post from Dodge Roll on 01/02/2015.

The Membrane is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon, Exit the Gungeon, and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. Notes: The Absent Minded synergy is a reference to the movie The Absent-Minded Professor, which features an extremely bouncy material known as Flubber. Due to its overpenetration, high range, and high projectile count, the Membrane works extremely well with Flak Bullets and Scattershot. This works even better with the synergy due to homing.

The Kiln is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires pots that break into 3 bouncing crystals upon hitting an enemy or object. Notes: This gun is a reference to The Legend of Zelda series, where thrown pots can be used to deal damage. The crystals that the pots break into are also a reference to rupees in The Legend of Zelda. Kiln is also an Anagram for Link, the protagonist of The Legend of Zelda.

Shock Rifle is a gun that fires a zap of electricity. It can electrify water, and grants immunity to electricity while held. Notes: Assault and Battery - If the player also has the M16, the guns are dual wielded. Battery Powered - If the player has R2G2 or Shock Rounds, Shock Rifle gains a smartphone battery-esque look and automatically regenerates 1 ammo every 4 seconds. When fired at a Gunjurer, the bullets are fired back at a slower speed. Instead of being fired back as a laser-like bullet, it now looks like a thunder bolt. Trivia: Removed: - If the player has Battery Bullets, shot speed is doubled. Likely removed because it includes a starting item.

Trashcannon is a gun that fires a trash bag, which explodes into 6 poison projectiles flying 60° apart from each other that create pools of poison. Notes: Garbage Collecting - If the player has Unity, hitting an enemy with Trashcannon causes it and its bullets to briefly freeze. Grouch - Junk increases the damage of the Trashcannon by 25%. This effect does not stack with multiple Junk (despite the Ammonomicon entry claiming otherwise). Recycling Bin - Starpew transforms the Trashcannon into a recycling bin, causing it to shoot high damage recycled shells instead of trash, and also greatly increasing its magazine...

Singularity is an active item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Spawns a black hole that sucks enemies, bullets, and items into it. Enemies in the black hole take damage. Bullets sucked into the black hole are destroyed. Notes: Bosses are not pulled into the black hole. Consumes any spawned in its proximity. Beam weapons can be used around the Singularity without being pulled in.

Laser Lotus is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires blue laser blasts capable of each piercing 1 enemy. These lasers electrify any water that they pass over. The Laser Lotus does not grant electric immunity, making electrified puddles of water potentially very dangerous to the player. Notes: This gun is a reference to Community. The character Pierce is a "Level 5 Laser Lotus" in his Buddhist renewal community.

Big Iron is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It fires a shotgun spread of 3ETG/2XTG bullets. Notes: ETG Despite Big Iron using bullets in its ammo bar, shell cartridges come out of its compartment when reloading. ETG Despite firing a spread of multiple bullets like a shotgun, the Big Iron is not in the SHOTGUN gun class, instead residing in the PISTOL class. This gun is a reference to the Marty Robbins song of the same name.

Amulet of the Pit Lord is a passive item. Effects: Falling into pits no longer deals damage. Trivia: The item is a reference to a famous Magic the Gathering card "Lord of the Pit." The "bargain" referred to in this text refers to the card mechanic whereby you must sacrifice your... The code for Amulet of the Pit Lord is stored in a class called 'RingOfPitFriendship', which may have been an in-development name/theme for the item. Since the player will still go through the falling animation the item can be used to avoid other sources of damage for a brief time.

Gundromeda Strain is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Reduces the health of all enemies and bosses by 25%. Notes: This item reduces the health of both normal enemies and bosses. Gundromeda Strain's effect is equivalent to increasing damage by 33%. Gundromeda Strain allows for quicker kills on bosses due to the bonus not being tied to DPS caps (where additional damage, otherwise, could be ignored).

Gunknight Helmet is a passive item. Effects: Grants two pieces of armor upon pickup. Grants a piece of armor upon entering a new floor. Notes: Cormorant - Acquiring all four Gunknight items transforms the player into Cormorant. Having this item increases the chance to find other Gunknight items. Trivia: The second paragraph of the Ammonomicon entry implies the Resourceful Rat stole his helmet.

Gunknight Greaves is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Grants a piece of Armor upon pickup. Grants a piece of Armor upon entering a new floor. Notes: Having this item increases the chance to find other Gunknight items. One of the projectiles that can be fired by Pedestal Mimics takes the form of a spinning Gunknight Greave.

Gunknight Gauntlet is a passive item. Effects: Grants a piece of armor upon pickup. Grants a piece of armor upon entering a new floor. Notes: Clawntlet - If the player has Cat Claw, Gunknight Gauntlet will grant 3 armor instead of 1, and taking damage causes bullets from the currently held gun to be sprayed in all direc... Cormorant - Acquiring all four Gunknight items transforms the player into Cormorant. Having this item increases the chance to find other Gunknight items.

Gunknight Armor is a passive item. Effects: Grants a piece of armor upon pickup. Grants a piece of armor upon entering a new floor. Notes: Cormorant - Acquiring all four Gunknight items transforms the player into Cormorant. Having this item increases the chance to find other Gunknight items. Dropping this item, then picking it up again will not produce more armor. Trivia: Unused: Constitution Check - (CustomSynergyType.ARMORED_GUNKNIGHT) If the player has AC-15, it causes an unknown effect.

Heart Synthesizer is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: If the player is not at full health, adds a 20% chance to gain a Half Heart upon completing a room without taking damage. Notes: Heart Synthesizer has no benefit for The Robot, as it cannot use hearts. It still does, however, contribute to the synergy mentioned above. CMV In the Chinese Mobile Version of Enter the Gungeon, the sprite of Heart Synthesizer has been changed to resemble the logo of Chinese game developer XD, the company responsible...
Oiled Cylinder is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. Effects: Decreases reload time by 50%.

Shelleton Key is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Allows any chest or locked door to be opened without using a Key. Increases Curse by 1 while held. Notes: The Shelleton Key can only substitute regular Keys, and cannot open locks that require special keys. This includes Cell locks (which require Cell Keys), the hatch to the Resourceful Rat's Lair (which requires the Gnawed Key), and Rat Chests/Locks (which require Rat Keys). The Shelleton Key cannot be used to purchase items from Flynt.

Bloody Eye is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Decreases enemy bullet speed by 15%. Notes: This item can be counterproductive; since bullets are slower than usual, the time the bullets are in midair is increased, meaning the player can run into bullets that would have a... This item's name, appearance and effects are a reference to particularly potent form of a drug named Red Eye in Cowboy Bebop which, when taken, could heighten the user's reaction... The Fear the Old Blood Synergy is a reference to the game Bloodborne, known for its use of blood vials and "trick" weapons.

Double Vision is an active item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: While active, doubles all bullets fired by the player, but reduces accuracy. Notes: If you have multiple active items, switching off Double Vision while active will end the effect prematurely. The two bullets fired can be desynced by using the item while the current gun is in-between shots. This is easiest to achieve with slow firing weapons like Sniper Rifle or A.W.P.. This item and its Ammonomicon quote are a reference to a scene in the 1993 film Tombstone.

Black Hole Gun is a gun that fires black holes which attract enemies and bullets. The black holes travel slowly across the room until they hit a wall. Notes: Cosmic Horror - If the player has Abyssal Tentacle, black holes fire three tentacles while active. The black hole will also attract friendly projectiles; however, beam type weapons will not be affected. The black hole will not attract projectiles that aren't moving, such as Blockner's shields. Trivia: The gun's Ammonomicon Entry is a reference to the 1994 song "Black Hole Sun" by the American rock band Soundgarden.

Ice Cube is a passive item. Effects: Allows active items to recharge while they are active. Increases Coolness by 3. Notes: Ice To Meet You - If the player also has Heart of Ice, firing with any gun will periodically fire a Heart of Ice projectile that splits and bounces. The probability of firing the ice projectile appears to be inversely proportional to the gun's fire rate. Rapidly clicking with a charge weapon will fire an ice projectile without consuming ammo. Trivia: The synergy Willing to Sacrifice - is a reference to the song "Cold As Ice" by Foreigner.

Ghost Bullets is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Bullets pierce through enemies and pass through obstacles (including tables). Each bullet can pierce through one enemy. Bullets that have pierced an enemy deal half damage.

Potion of Gun Friendship is an active item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: While active, increases damage by 30%, doubles fire rate, decreases reload times by 70%, and multiplies knockback by 10. Notes: Switching to another active item while the Potion of Gun Friendship is active will prematurely end the effect. Fixed Bug Prior to the A Farewell to Arms update, if the player were to drop Potion of Gun Friendship while the Friend to Gun and Bullet was active, the copy gun became permanent. The copied gun was carried over to the Past.

Tangler is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires shotgun blasts of rectangular bullets that bounce off walls. Enemies hit will gain a rectangular appearance. Notes: Despite being a shotgun, the Tangler is in the SILLY gun class. This means that unlike most other shotguns, the Tangler is not affected by the Shotgun Affinity synergy. Jammed enemies, the Bookllet variant in the R&G Dept., and the ghost of Blockner are unaffected by Tangler's visual distortion effect.

Gungeon Ant is a gun that alternates between firing spreads of bullets that leave behind oil and ones that burn enemies and ignite oil upon reloading. Notes: This gun is a reference to the real-life Bullet Ant. Known for its extremely painful sting, victims have likened it to "being shot". The Great Queen Ant synergy is a reference to Earthworm Jim, specifically The Evil Queen Pulsating, Bloated, Festering, Sweaty, Pus-filled, Malformed, Slug-for-a-Butt.

Alien Engine is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Rapidly fires tiny invisible pellets with a very short range, extending only slightly past the gun's visible muzzle flash. Sets enemies on Fire (Dur: 4s DPS: 4). Pushes the player backwards, away from the direction aimed, while firing. Notes: Despite its downsides, this gun has among the highest DPS in the Enter the Gungeon, making it useful for hit-and-run tactics in a Boss battle, or in combination with a longer rang... In September of 2016, Dodge Roll programmer Brent Sodman listed the Alien Engine as his favourite gun in Enter the Gungeon, and Shotgrub as his least favourite.

Crestfaller is a gun that fires bullets which freeze enemies. Notes: Crestfallen Soul - If the player also has Gun Soul, dodge roll speed is increased by 20% and Crestfaller deals 50% more damage if the player dies and has not yet retrieved their s... Five O'Clock Somewhere - If the player also has Shotga Cola, Shotgun Coffee, Double Vision, Potion of Gun Friendship, Potion of Lead Skin, or Old Knight's Flask, reloading will fi... As with Gungine, pressing the reload button while the clip is full will still clear liquids. Trivia: Altered: Diazepam - Prior to the Advanced Gungeons & Draguns Update, this gun was included in Diazepam.

Proton Backpack is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires two slow-moving simultaneous beams; A thick homing (Rad: 18 Vel: 420) red laser that deals very low damage.
