A thin electric homing (Rad: 15 Vel: 420) beam that deals the majority of the Proton Backpack's DPS. Notes: This gun is a reference to the iconic Proton Pack ghost fighting weapon from the Ghostbusters franchise. The Ammonomicon quote "Crossing Streams" is in reference to the repeated warning in Ghostbusters (1984) "Don't cross the streams", as crossing the streams from multiple Proton Bac...

Grasschopper is a very tiny gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires powerful blasts that have very strong knockback and delete bullets, like a small Blank effect. Notes: The explosions created on impact are not blanks, despite being able to delete bullets. They will not open secret rooms or trigger Ammolets. If a Gunjurer catches and returns a shot from this gun, the blank effect will still damage the player. This weapon is a reference to the Noisy Cricket from Men in Black.

Winchester Rifle is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: The Winchester Rifle appears to be based off the real world Winchester Model 1866, the first 'Winchester Rifle' created by the Winchester Repeating Arms Company. Although classified as semi-automatic, the Winchester Rifle is functionally automatic, as its rate of fire does not benefit from tapping the fire button instead of holding it down. The Ammonomicon description "Guns and Deviltry" is a direct reference to Swords and Deviltry, the first collected volume of Fafhrd and the Gray Mouser short stories.

Grey Mauser is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Stealths the player upon reloading an empty clip. While stealthed, enemies do not see the player, and they may steal from merchants. Deals 400% damage if fired while the bearer is stealthed. Notes: Followers like Wingman will not shoot while the invisibility effect is active. This gun's name is a portmanteau of an early semi-automatic pistol design, the Mauser C96, and the thief from Fritz Leiber's fantasy books known as the Gray Mouser. This gun shares its name with the Grey Mauser, the NPC alongside Frifle, from which you unlock this gun.

Ser Manuel's Revolver is an unobtainable gun in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: Ser Manuel's Revolver is wielded by Ser Manuel in his bossfight in the Halls of Knowledge. Due to the way enemy AI is coded in Enter the Gungeon, companions and enemies which use weapons disconnected from their sprite hold real guns which are theoretically usable in the... However, the fact that the Ser Manuel's Revolver has a unique description in the Ammonomicon suggests that it may have at one point been intended for player use.

The Judge is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: The last shot of each magazine will fire one of the following special projectiles, chosen at random; A green bullet that deals 4 damage, Poisons (Dur: 2.5s DPS: 3) enemies, and leaves a small puddle of poison goop. A flare that deals 4 damage and sets enemies on Fire (Dur: 4s DPS: 4). Notes: This gun is a reference to Judge Dredd's gun, the Lawgiver. The pickup quote "Hot Shot" is one of the Lawgiver's settings. The entry for The Judge in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of a round-faced figure holding a gun.

Machine Fist is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Upon reloading, the Machine Fist alternates modes between a fast firing minigun and a single shot fist-shaped rocket. With keyboard and mouse controls, the rocket fist will home in towards the players cursor. With gamepad controls, the rocket will update its direction to match the currently held aim direction on the controller. Notes: Like other single shot weapons, firing a rocket fist causes the player to reload the gun immediately after, transforming it. In order to reload, the gun must have remaining ammo greater than the size of the magazine.

Disarming Personality is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. The Pilot starts with this item in both games. Effects: Grants a 15% discount at shops.ETG Grants a 20% discount at shops.XTG Notes: Cannot be sold, even as gungeoneers other than The Pilot. Does not provide a discount for the Gnawed Key or the Prime Primer. If Disarming Personality is bought from a shop, the discount will apply to itself.

Rolling Eye is a passive item. Effects: Rolling through bullets reflects them at enemies. Reflected bullets deal 10 damage. Notes: Fear The Old Blood - If the player has Trick Gun, its reload time is decreased by 33% and its fire rate is increased by 20%. Iroquois - If the player has MAC10, its reload time is decreased by 33%, its magazine size is increased by 50%, and its knockback is increased by 50%. If one player on co-op has MAC10 and another player has Rolling Eye, the player who has Rolling Eye equipped will gain the benefits of the synergy for all guns.
Ring of Fire Resistance is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Grants immunity to fire.

Bug Boots is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Dodge rolling leaves a trail of poison goop. Grants immunity to poison. Notes: Use of this item allows the player to spread poison over the entrance to Bullet Hell, despite there visually being no ground to spread it on.

Fossilized Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon It fires a stream of oil goop. Reloading causes the skull to spit fire, which can ignite the oil. Notes: The plume of flame belched by the Fossilized Gun when reloading solely has the effect of igniting flammable goop. Is is possible to pre-clear rooms by firing blindly into them from the doorway without fully entering, and then igniting the placed oil. This gun is a combined reference to dinosaur fossils, fossil fuels (ie; oil, so named because it is formed from organic remains), and fire-breathing dragons.

Pea Shooter is a Gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It shoots low-damage peas. Notes: A peashooter is a type of toy blowgun. The term "pea shooter" is also used derogatorily to describe a weak, small-caliber firearm. The Pea Shooter is given to you by Ser Manuel in the tutorial. The Pea Shooter may be a reference to the Plants Vs.

Gunslinger's Ashes is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires spectral gunslingers that slowly walk forwards and home (Rad: 10 Vel: 180) towards nearby enemies. Every 2 seconds, these gunslingers will fire a bullet at the nearest enemy which deals 5 damage. These spirits typically dissipate after shooting three times. The damage dealt by the spirits themselves making contact with enemies is considerably higher than that of their shots. Notes: Due to the high damage dealt by the spirits themselves making contact with enemies, it is most effective to fire this gun at close range.

Luxin Cannon is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon which rapidly fires small, solidified beams of light at enemies. When the magazine is emptied, the tip of the crystal will be launched. Notes: This gun is a reference to The Lightbringer book series by Brent Weeks. "Falsely rumored to have natural healing abilities" is a reference to crystal healing, a pseudoscientific alternative medicine technique that employs stones and crystals. The The Return synergy is a reference to Ganon from the Legend of Zelda video game series.

Charmed Bow is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: The Charmed Bow has two different levels of charge. The lower level, requiring the gun to be charged for only 0.2 seconds, fires a mundane arrow. The higher level, requiring the gun to be charged for 0.7 seconds, fires a faster moving and more damaging arrow with a heart trail. Notes: Shots fired from the Orange Guon Stone and Space Friend while the Unbelievably Charming synergy is active are guaranteed to charm enemies. The entry for the Charmed Bow in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of a winged Bullet Kin in the role of Cupid, carrying the weapon.

Portable Turret is an active item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Places a stationary turret that shoots at enemies and blocks enemy projectiles. The turret will be destroyed if it takes enough damage. The turret will disappear after the room has been cleared or enough time has passed. Notes: If the player has the M1911 with Pulp Gungeon, Portable Turret's shots will make the M1911 circling around the player fire. The effect stacks; if there are multiple Portable Turrets firing at once, the circling M1911 will shoot for each one firing. If the player also has Bumbullets, bees will be shot out of the circling M1911 at a very fast rate while Portable Turret is firing.

The Sawed-Off is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon, where it serves as The Convict's secondary starting gun. It is a shotgun that fires four short-range bullets with low accuracy. Notes: The Sawed-Off is in the SHOTGUN gun class, and as such benefits from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. This also means that, according to the rules of how loot is distributed, holding the Sawed-Off makes finding other SHOTGUN type weapons more rare. Notably, this makes it the only starting weapon to technically be part of a synergy.

Cigarettes is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Deals half a heart of damage to the player. This functions exactly the same as all other damage sources in the game, and will break Armor first. Increases Coolness by 1 per use, decreasing the cooldown of active items and increasing the chance of items dropping upon clearing a room. Discards a cigarette onto the floor. If this cigarette lands in flammable goop such as oil or Phaser Spider web, it will ignite it. Notes: Dying to Cigarettes lists "Smoking" as the cause of death on the Ammonomicon death page. Using Cigarettes during a boss fight will count as taking damage, and will prevent the player from obtaining a Master Round.

Irradiated Lead is a passive item and bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Adds a chance to fire green bullets that poison enemies for 2.5 seconds. The chance for any given projectile to be poisoned is 50% multiplied by the effect chance scalar of the players current gun. Beam-style guns have a flat 25% chance per second to poison enemies they are shooting. Notes: If an explosive projectile (such as from the RPG or Explosive Rounds) is poisoned by Irradiated Lead, it will leave a small pool of poison goop upon exploding. Emerald Weapon is a reference to, and named after, one of the superbosses in the game Final Fantasy VII.

Poison Vial is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Throws a vial which breaks open to create a puddle of poison goop. Notes: If used in the Gorguns boss room, the vial will not shatter upon hitting the floor, instead sinking harmlessly into the water without releasing any poison.

Charm Horn is an active item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Upon use, charms enemies within an 8 tile radius around the player for 10 seconds. Charms all merchant NPCs within an 8 tile radius of the player for 10 seconds. While charmed, the player can steal from that merchant. Notes: Hornucopia - If the player has Banana, the exploding banana will send out five bouncing explosive bananas instead of three, one of which explodes into 4 additional exploding banan... If used on the Misfire Beast's projection, the projection will home in on the beast and instantly kill it. Does not affect bosses. Trivia: This item is similar to Boromir's horn in The Lord of the Rings.

Plague Pistol is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Bullets fired from the Plague Pistol have a 50% chance to inflict the poison effect on enemies. These bullets also leave a small pool of lingering poison goop on the ground when destroyed. Notes: Because bullets from the Plague Pistol leave non-flammable goop on the ground, they can be used to extinguish chest fuses and the fireplace. The entry for the Plague Pistol in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of a Mutant Bullet Kin.

Plunger is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: The Plunger fires a constant stream of green liquid that inflicts the poison status effect on enemies after 0.5 seconds of contact with the beam. Reloading the Plunger while standing on any goop, including goop that it has created, fully restores the guns ammo. Notes: The phrase Médecins Sans Diplôme in the description is French for Doctors Without Diplomas. The Plunger can be seen used by The Last Human for her poison spray attack in the Robots past. Bug The diacritic marks on é and ô are not supported by Enter the Gungeons font, causing "Médecins Sans Diplôme" to display as "Mdecins Sans Diplme" when read ingame.

Sense of Direction is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Upon use, an arrow appears that points in the direction of the floor's exit. Notes: Using this item in the Resourceful Rat's Lair will cause the arrow to point in a random direction each time. This item doesn't work in the R&G Dept., possibly because it has the same layout every time, and the player doesn't leave this floor through an elevator. Combined with Blank Companion's Ring or Ring of Triggers, Sense of Direction allows for more frequent activation of their powerful effects.

Gunbow is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires fast-moving bullets that bounce seven times. Notes: Enemies killed by the gunbow are vaporised, and leave no corpse behind. The name of this gun could be a reference to the Monster Hunter series, which features Bowguns.

Ballistic Boots is a passive item. Effects: Increases movement speed by 2. Notes: Knight Time - If the player has Gunknight Greaves, taking damage briefly stops time. Triple Jump - If paired with Springheel Boots, this item grants a third roll to the two already available. Trivia: The "pair of boots that fire bullets" mentioned in the Ammonomicon entry are the Gunboots.

Lichy Trigger Finger is a passive item. Effects: Increases rate of fire by 25%. Notes: Liches Get Stitches - If the player has Riddle of Lead, killing an enemy grants a temporary fire rate boost, signified by a green arrow above the player's head like those above en... If duplicates of this item are somehow obtained, further increases manual fire rate. Trivia: The synergy name "Liches Get Stitches" is a reference to the phrase "snitches get stitches". The item's name is a play on the phrase "itchy trigger finger". In Dungeons & Dragons, a phylactery is a magical item into which a lich binds its soul. As long as its phylactery is intact, a lich cannot be permanently destroyed.

Coin Crown is a passive item in both Enter and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Has a 10% chance to give 5 upon clearing a room without taking damage. Notes: It is easy to unlock with the Briefcase of Cash. If the player already has 50 then the 250 will immediately unlock it. Obtaining the Gold Junk will automatically unlock it. This is because the Gold Junk grants the player 500 when picked up. If duplicates of this item are somehow obtained, increases the chance of a 5 shell spawning upon clearing a room and allows multiple Coin Crowns to be activated at once.

Box is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: While wearing the box, enemies will not target the player. Firing a shot while under the box deals double damage and removes the box. Reloading does not remove the box, even if the player has Hip Holster. Notes: Has no effect if used while enemies are already targeting the player. While it has no effect on regular bosses as already stated, you can use this to be hidden for the first phase of the Resourceful Rat’s boss fight. Stealth can be used before entering the boss room but cannot be activated while fighting a boss.

Old Knight's Shield is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Grants two pieces of Armor. Notes: This item is obtained in a secret room in the Halls of Knowledge. To find it, go to the room on the left of the first room after Manuel leaves the player. Talking to Ser Manuel with Old Knight's Helm and Old Knight's Shield after defeating him unlocks the Blockner fight. Once obtained in the Halls of Knowledge, the player will 'unlock' the Old Knight's Shield.

AK-47 is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon, Exit the Gungeon, and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. In the Apple Arcade version of Exit the Gungeon, the AK-47 is called the AP-13. Notes: The AK-47 is wielded by some Bullet KinETG, TankersETG, and HollowpointsXTG. ETG If the player feeds the AK-47 and either the Flame Hand or Megahand to a regular Muncher, it will always spit out a Machine Fist. The description of the AK-47 is a reference to the film Jackie Brown.

Old Knight's Helm is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Grants two pieces of Armor. Notes: This item is obtained in a secret room in the Hall of Knowledge. To find it, go to the room on the left of the first room after Manuel leaves the player. Talking to Ser Manuel with Old Knight's Helm and Old Knight's Shield after defeating him unlocks the Blockner fight. Once obtained in the Halls of Knowledge, the player will 'unlock' the Old Knight's Helm.

Cold 45 is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon, Exit the Gungeon, and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. Its bullets can freeze enemies. Notes: Its name is a reference to the term "Colt 45" (which is likely the "typographical error" that the Ammonomicon Entry refers to). "Shatterday Night Special" is a reference to the term "Saturday Night Special", a nickname for a cheap, easily disposable handgun normally purchased in a hasty decision to commit... The yeti in the Why's Bullet's Crying? synergy may be a reference to the yetis seen in Fork Parker's holiday profit hike.

Blanks are important pickups that appear in Enter the Gungeon, Exit the Gungeon, and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. Notes: Blanks have a unique icon on the map, showing up as It is never explained how Blanks function in-universe, with various dialogues and ammonomicon entries suggesting they emit a sonic frequency, while others state that they send bul... Blanks, like other pickups, can be carried over in the the pasts of various Gungeoneers, potentially offering an advantage if the player is able to amass a stockpile.

Ice Breaker is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: The first two shots of each magazine fire a shotgun blast of freezing ice bullets. The final shot of each magazine fires a single large, metal slug that can pierce up to 5 times. This slug will also pass through breakables without being stopped. Notes: Because the ice shots of the Ice Breaker use the Ice damage type, they deal 1.5x damage to Muzzle Wisps and Muzzle Flares, and do not ignite Coalers. Despite the Ammonomicon entry suggesting otherwise, the metal slugs fired by this gun do not deal any more damage to frozen enemies. Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Ice Breaker had 100 maximum ammo.

Wristbow is an unobtainable gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon that fires fast arrows, similar to the Crossbow. Notes: ETG This gun is used by Skullets, Skullmets, and Knight Bullet Kin. Due to the way enemy AI is coded in Enter the Gungeon, companions and enemies which use weapons disconnected from their sprite hold real guns which are theoretically usable in the... However, the fact that the Wristbow has a unique description in the Ammonomicon suggests that it may have at one point been intended for player use.

Particulator is a gun that fires a large bullet, which then splits into 10 smaller bullets that seek out enemies. Notes: Particle Accelerator - If the player also has Laser Sight, whenever a smaller bullet hits an enemy or object, a laser is automatically fired in its direction. Upon splitting, the large bullet creates a visual blast effect that is similar to a blank but doesn't have any actual effect. The homing projectiles are extremely useful for clearing large numbers of enemies, and for locating enemies off-screen. Trivia: The gun is a reference to a particle accelerator, with the "shower of exotic particles" referring to a Particle shower.

Hegemony Carbine is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. This weapon rapidly shoots bright, white bolts. Notes: The tagline "All The Same" is possibly a reference to the Rifleman's Creed. This gun is used in The Marine's past. It's possible to bring the Galactic Medal of Valor over to his past, which will allow you to acquire the Decorated synergy.

Helix is a gun that fires two waving bullets. Notes: Double Double Helix - If the player also has Helix Bullets, instead of the Helix Bullets effect being applied to each bullet, Helix's projectiles will be doubled (2 bullets will f... The Helix is a surprisingly powerful weapon, though difficult to use against regular enemies due to the way it fires. If the player also has Remote Bullets, the spread of the two bullets is reduced as they follow the crosshair, making it easier to use on smaller enemies. Trivia: The gun likely refers to DNA, which has a double helix shape that is similar to the bullets' path. The phrase "Splice n' Dice" is a reference to gene splicing.

Gilded Hydra is a gun that fires large bursts of piercing, bouncing bullets. Its magazine size is increased by 1 for each half heart the player is missing, and healing any missing health decreases magazine size. Notes: Ecstasy of Gold - If the player also has Gilded Bullets, Gilded Hydra's damage is increased by 25%. The Gilded Hydra is part of the SHOTGUN gun class, and as such benefits from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. Magazine size will not increase when playing as The Robot. Trivia: The decreasing health adding to the clip size is an allusion to the Lernaean Hydra of Greek mythology, a beast that would sprout two heads in the place of one that was severed.

Space Friend is a passive item and companion in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Follows the player and fires lasers at enemies which deal 5 damage. Blocks enemy bullets. Notes: Lasers fired by the Space Friend can be caught by Gunjurers. The Space Friend is a reference to the arcade shoot-em-up series Raiden. Bug Like other orbitals or orbital companions, the Space Friend may take a few moments to catch up to the player when entering the Aimless Void.

iBomb Companion App is an active item. Effects: Detonates all explosives, explosive enemies, and exploding enemy bullets currently present in the room. Notes: Enemies detonated include: Bombshee Det Trivia: The item is a reference to the popular iPhone and iPad Apple products and their many diverse applications (usually refereed to as "Apps"). The Ammonomicon's quote for the item "One For That" is a reference to the slogan used to advertise these aforementioned products, that slogan being "There's an App for that!". The appearance of this item is a reference to the Pip Boy device from the Fallout franchise.

Aged Bell is an active item in Enter the Gungeon and a Powerup in Exit the Gungeon. Notes: Using the Aged Bell is one simple strategy for unlocking Shelleton Key. This item may be a reference to the Unheard Bell from the manga series Made In Abyss, which is also a magical bell which stops time. It may also be a reference to the magical necromantic bell Saraneth from the Abhorsen series of books by Garth Nix.

Duct Tape is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: When used on two different guns sequentially, they will be taped together and combined. Additional projectiles, taken from the consumed gun, fired alongside the host gun's base projectiles. Additional ammo capacity, equal to the consumed gun's ammo capacity. Notes: Combined guns still only count as one gun when fed to a Muncher. Duct Tape may be a reference to the movie Aliens, In which the protagonist Ripley tapes together an AR and flamethrower near the end of the film while preparing for a rescue.

Crutch is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: All bullets fired by the player slightly home (Rad: 5 Vel: 250) in towards enemies. Notes: The homing of this item appears to stack with guns whose projectiles already home, allowing them to turn faster towards their targets. Similarly, if duplicates of this item are somehow obtained, bullets home in on enemies more strongly, turning faster to reach their target. Heavy Bullets works well with this item if the player plans to rely on the homing, giving bullets more airtime to home in on enemies.

Scattershot is a passive item and a bullet upgrade. Effects: Guns fire triple the projectiles, with each projectile dealing 55% of its original damage. Notes: Dumb Smart Bullets - If the player also has Homing Bullets, bullets that don't home in become larger and deal 50% more damage. Laser Light Show - If the player also has Laser Rifle, Laser Rifle becomes blue and gold, and fires 3 sets of 3 shots in a spread per trigger pull. Each shot bounces once. If all projectiles hit, the damage is effectively increased by 65%.

Napalm Strike is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Sets a large rectangular area on fire, igniting and damaging enemies. Notes: Altered: Napalm B - This synergy used to only let Mega Douser spread Napalm Strike's fire. It can now spread all fire. The category "Smells Great!" in the Ammonomicon is a reference to a quote from the movie Apocalypse Now, "I love the smell of napalm in the morning." Prior to the A Farewell to Arms Update, Napalm Strike was a item.

Infuriating Note is a passive item. It is a note containing a mocking poem written by the Resourceful Rat. It is found randomly in any type of chest, aside from the sixth one which can only be dropped from a Mimic. Effects: Has six variants, each of which reveals one of the directions necessary to make it through the maze in the Resourceful Rat's Lair. Notes: Each note has an unlock condition that must be met before they can appear: First note: Beat Gungeon Proper. Second note: Have an item get stolen by the Resourceful Rat.

Infuriating Note is a passive item. It is a note containing a mocking poem written by the Resourceful Rat. It is found randomly in any type of chest, aside from the sixth one which can only be dropped from a Mimic. Effects: Has six variants, each of which reveals one of the directions necessary to make it through the maze in the Resourceful Rat's Lair. Notes: Each note has an unlock condition that must be met before they can appear: First note: Beat Gungeon Proper. Second note: Have an item get stolen by the Resourceful Rat.

Infuriating Note is a passive item. It is a note containing a mocking poem written by the Resourceful Rat. It is found randomly in any type of chest, aside from the sixth one which can only be dropped from a Mimic. Effects: Has six variants, each of which reveals one of the directions necessary to make it through the maze in the Resourceful Rat's Lair. Notes: Each note has an unlock condition that must be met before they can appear: First note: Beat Gungeon Proper. Second note: Have an item get stolen by the Resourceful Rat.

It is found randomly in any type of chest, aside from the sixth one which can only be dropped from a Mimic. Effects: Has six variants, each of which reveals one of the directions necessary to make it through the maze in the Resourceful Rat's Lair. Notes: Each note has an unlock condition that must be met before they can appear: First note: Beat Gungeon Proper. Second note: Have an item get stolen by the Resourceful Rat.

Infuriating Note is a passive item. It is a note containing a mocking poem written by the Resourceful Rat. It is found randomly in any type of chest, aside from the sixth one which can only be dropped from a Mimic. Effects: Has six variants, each of which reveals one of the directions necessary to make it through the maze in the Resourceful Rat's Lair. Notes: Each note has an unlock condition that must be met before they can appear: First note: Beat Gungeon Proper. Second note: Have an item get stolen by the Resourceful Rat.

Infuriating Note is a passive item. It is a note containing a mocking poem written by the Resourceful Rat. It is found randomly in any type of chest, aside from the sixth one which can only be dropped from a Mimic. Effects: Has six variants, each of which reveals one of the directions necessary to make it through the maze in the Resourceful Rat's Lair. Notes: Each note has an unlock condition that must be met before they can appear: First note: Beat Gungeon Proper. Second note: Have an item get stolen by the Resourceful Rat.

Owl is a passive item and companion in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Summons an owl companion that flies close behind the player, blocking enemy bullets. Has a chance to activate a miniature Blank effect when enemy bullets come near the player. Occasionally shoots a single bullet at a random enemy. Notes: The owl's blanks will not open Secret Rooms. Owl's bullets are affected by all bullet modifiers. The Special Delivery synergy is a reference to the Harry Potter franchise, where owls are used to deliver mail and packages between wizards.

Grappling Hook is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Allows players to hook onto and then pull themselves towards walls at a fast speed. The hook has unlimited range. Hitting an enemy with the hook Stuns it and deals 10 damage. Notes: If the player cannot reach the hook's location (e.g. table blocking the way), the hook will automatically deactivate after a few seconds. The Grappling Hook can be used to cheat in Winchester's Games. Using Grappling Hook to steal items while inside the shop will sometimes cause the player to get caught, even on the first theft. Trivia: This item is a reference to the Hookshot, an item from the The Legend of Zelda franchise.

The Prize Pistol is a gun used only for Winchester's minigames in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: If the player leaves the shooting range mid-game, the Prize Pistol will be removed from their inventory and they will not receive a reward. Although the max ammo of the Prize Pistol is 1, Winchester loads four shots into the gun before the minigame begins, resulting in normally inaccessible ammo values of 4/1, 3/1, an... While the Prize Pistol does not appear in Exit the Gungeon, its sprites and description are present in the games assets.

Air Strike is an active item. Effects: Calls down a series of explosions over a long rectangular area. Each explosion deals 25 damage. Notes: Air Support - If the player has Explosive Rounds, Air Strike will cover a massive area. Shot Across The Bow - If the player has Gunbow, it fires explosive missiles.

Gungeon Pepper is a passive item. Effects: Continuously deals damage to nearby enemies at a rate of 5 damage per second. Notes: Maximize Spell - If the player has Flame Hand, it turns green, and green flames will leave pools of green fire upon hitting enemies. Sausage and Pepper - If the player has Meatbun, Gungeon Pepper's range will be doubled. The synergy is lost when Meatbun is consumed. Phoenix Up - If the player has Phoenix, changes the look of the gun and summons a Phoenix companion that fires at enemies.

Ring of Chest Friendship is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Increases the chance of finding rewards upon clearing rooms. Increases the chance of finding a chest upon clearing a room by 50%. Modifies the chances for chests spawned on room clear to be a specific quality; Notes: If duplicates of this item are somehow obtained, they will not stack their effects.

Ancient Hero's Bandana is a passive item. Effects: Quadruples maximum ammo. Notes: I need scissors! 61! What A Thrill - If the player also has Patriot, it has a chance to transmogrify enemies into snakes. Has no effect on the Prize Pistol. Trivia: Unused: Recessive Hero - (CustomSynergyType.THE_TRUE_HERO) If the player has Number 2, it causes an unknown effect. Removed because it contains a starting item. This item is a reference to the Metal Gear Solid series. Ancient Hero's Bandana has the same sprite shape as Bloodied Scarf and Number 2.

"If only I had some heavy boots, I wouldn't slip on this ice!" - Lost Adventurer Heavy Boots is a passive item in Enter and Exit the Gungeon. It also appears in powerup form in Exit the Gungeon. Effects: ETG Prevents the players movement from being interfered with by external forces, such as knockback from enemies, gun recoil, conveyor belts, and patches of ice. XTG Causes the player to release a shockwave upon landing. Enemies caught in the shockwave will be knocked back and take light damage. Has a short cooldown before the effect can be activated again. Notes: XTG This item can be very useful on elevators with holes.

Broccoli is a passive item. Effects: Increases damage by 10%. Adds a 10% chance to negate damage. Increases movement speed by 1. Notes: If duplicates of this item are somehow obtained, further increases damage, the chance to negate damage, and movement speed. Prior to the Supply Drop UpdateETG, Broccoli was an item, did not increase speed, and only gave a 5% damage increase. The Ammonomicon entry for Broccoli is almost identical to the first sentence on the Wikipedia page for Broccoli, which reads "Broccoli is an edible green plant in the cabbage fami...

Antibody is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Adds a 50% chance to heal the player for an extra half a heart whenever they receive healing. Notes: In the real world, antibodies are proteins produced by the immune system to assist in fighting infection.

Pink Guon Stone is a passive item and a Guon Stone in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Grants one extra heart container. Orbits the player, blocking enemy shots on contact. Notes: Has no effect on the health of The Robot. The Guon Stones are a reference to Ioun Stones in Dungeons & Dragons. The visual appearance of this item may also be a reference to Life Crystals in the game Terraria.

White Guon Stone is a passive item and a Guon Stone. Effects: Grants an extra blank per floor. Orbits the player, blocking enemy shots on contact. Notes: Tile Match - If the player also has Tetrominator, whenever the player shoots, White Guon Stone will occasionally shoot a yellow cube Tetromino itself. Whiter Guon Stone - If the player also has +1 Bullets, Amulet of the Pit Lord, or Flash Ray, the size of the Guon Stone is increased, it rotates at a fixed distance from the playe... Trivia: The White Guon Stone, like all the other guon stones, is based on the Ioun Stones, popularized by Dungeons and Dragons.

Orange Guon Stone is a passive item and a Guon Stone. Effects: Shoots at enemies. Each shot deals 5 damage. Has a cooldown of 1 second between shots. Notes: If the player has both the Enter the Fruitgeon and Oranger Guon Stone synergies at the same time, their damage increases do not stack. This item benefits from the Y.V. shrine, for a chance to fire extra bullets. This item benefits from Battle Standard's damage boost.

Clear Guon Stone is a passive item and a Guon Stone in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Negates poison damage. Orbits the player, blocking enemy shots on contact. Notes: The Clear Guon Stone, like all the other guon stones, is based on Ioun Stones, popularized by Dungeons and Dragons. The Clear Guon Stone resembles a Skullet. This item's use of the word clear, its subtext "pure" and the blue tint of the item are cumulatively a reference to the street drug methamphetamine, or Crystal Meth, and in partic...

Old Knight's Flask is an active item. Effects: Heals the player for one heart upon use. Starts with 2 charges, and refills to 2 charges at the start of each floor. It does not charge through damage. Notes: The player is immobile while the item is being used, and the player is not healed until the item is finished being used. The player is still vulnerable to damage while healing, so it is not recommended for in-combat recovery. This item does not heal The Robot. Trivia: This item is a reference to the Estus Flask from Dark Souls.

Red Guon Stone is a passive item and a Guon Stone in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Increases the speed and distance of dodge rolls. Orbits the player, blocking enemy shots on contact. Notes: For the purposes of Really Special Lute, Red Guon Stone counts as a companion.

Blue Guon Stone is a passive item and a Guon Stone in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Upon taking damage, decreases enemy shot speed by 33% for 5 seconds. Orbits the player, blocking enemy shots on contact. The stone orbits around the player faster than the other Guon Stones. Notes: Since the Farewell to Arms update, the Blue Guon Stone now rotates twice as fast compared to other Guon Stones, and therefore no longer affects the location of other Guon Stones t... If duplicates of this item are somehow obtained, further decreases enemy's shot speed and adds another Blue Guon Stone to the player. The Blue Guon Stone, like all the other guon stones, is based on the Ioun Stone, popularized by Dungeons and Dragons.

Riddle of Lead is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Grants a heart container. Fully heals the player. Increases damage by 30%. Notes: Dropping and picking up the item will not heal the player again. The chance to negate damage applies to falling damage from walking into pits. Negated damage will not prevent the player from earning a Master Round.

Iron Coin is an active item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Upon use, kills all enemies in a random unexplored room of the current floor. Where the effect took place, a note can be found lying in a pool of blood on the ground. While held: Notes: The player can get money from enemies killed in this way, but pickup drops will likely be stolen by the Rat before the player reaches the room. Chests will spawn normally after clearing rooms with this item. This item cannot clear boss rooms. Trivia: This item, including the amount of uses it has, is a reference to A Song of Ice and Fire, popularized as the television show, Game of Thrones.

Laser Sight is a passive item. Effects: Reduces weapon spread by 50%, increasing accuracy. Adds a laser sight that points where the player is aiming. Notes: Future Gangster - If the player has Thompson Sub-Machinegun, it fires lasers and increases damage by 1. Particle Accelerator - If the player has Particulator, whenever a split Particulator shot dissipates, a laser is automatically fired in its direction. Particle Flow - If the player also has RPG, Stinger, Zorgun, or Laser Lotus, crosshairs will appear on enemies and the guns will home in on them. Trivia: The description title is a reference to King of The Dot, the Canadian rap battle league of the same name.

Dark Marker is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: When uncharged, fires black and white projectiles with no special properties. When charged, fires two capsules (one matter, one antimatter) in a helical formation, arcing towards one another. Firing a charged shot empties the entire magazine of the gun, and consumes 10 ammo rather than 1. Notes: This gun does not anger Bello when fired in the shop unless it is charged. Despite being listed as a semiautomatic weapon in the Ammonomicon, the Dark Marker is one of the few guns in Enter the Gungeon to have multiple different shooting styles at once.

Flare Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires flares which set enemies on Fire (Dur: 4s DPS: 4). Notes: After hitting a wall, fired flares will continue burning for a time. These lingering flares produce light, but have no combat utility. The Synergy with Medkit may be a reference to how flare guns and medical supplies are often kept together for emergency situations. The quote “Over Here” may be a reference to the original Jurassic Park film.

Spice is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: If Spice has been used, it has a chance to replace future item drops depending on how many times it has been used. The effects of spice change depending on how many times it has been used. On the first use: Notes: In co-op mode, effects of spice usage are counted separately for each player. Possible Bug Dying in co-op mode removes any stat modifiers that the dead player gained through spice. These stats are not returned on being resurrected. The health reduction from Spice cannot put the player below 1 heart container, however continuing to put the health stat into the negatives by Spice usage will prevent future heal...

Fat Bullets is a passive item and bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Doubles bullet size. Increases damage by 30%. Decreases maximum ammo by 10%. Notes: The increased projectile size negatively affects guns with large projectiles such as the Crestfaller. The decrease in maximum ammo can be bypassed if you temporarily drop the item before picking up ammo drops or new guns. May be a reference to The Binding of Isaac, where many items that increase damage also increase tear size.

Frost Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade. Effects: Adds a chance to fire light-blue bullets that freeze enemies. The chance is higher for weapons with slower rates of fire. Notes: Added Effect - Ice - If the player has Alpha Bullet, the first shot of each magazine is guaranteed to be an ice bullet. Arctic Warfare - If the player has A.W.P., it turns orange and white, deals 62.5% more damage, and its shots are guaranteed to freeze enemies. Cold Ones - If the player has Frost Ammolet, the chance to fire ice bullets is significantly increased.

Super Hot Watch is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Slows down time depending on how slow the player is moving. When standing completely still, time is slowed by 99%. One second of normal time takes 1 minute and 40 seconds of standing still with the Super Hot Watch. Dodge rolling is always considered moving, even if the player is rolling against an obstacle such as a wall. This can be used to progress time while remaining in one place. Notes: While time is slowed, gun cooldowns are also slowed, making firing difficult. The five Master Rounds required to unlock the Super Hot Watch do not have to be unique. As such, duplicate Master Rounds acquired via Clone will still count towards the unlock.

Drum Clip is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Increases the magazine size of all weapons by 50%. ETG Does not affect guns with only one round in each magazine, as numbers are rounded down. XTG Single-round guns gain an extra bullet. Notes: Drum Clip can negatively affect weapons that have special effects on the final shot, such as Eye of the Beholster. If duplicates of this item are somehow obtained, further increases guns' magazine size. Doesn't affect guns with one round.

Gungeon Blueprint is a passive item. Effects: Fully maps out every subsequent floor. Reveals all secret rooms. Notes: Does not reveal the trapdoor leading to the Resourceful Rat's Lair. Trivia: This item is linked to Woban, the character mentioned in the Ammonomicon entry for the Cartographer's Ring. The Gungeon Blueprint's pickup quote references how the Gungeon is procedurally generated ingame.

Homing Bullets is a passive item and bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Bullets have a 20% chance to home (Rad: 10 Vel: 420) in on enemies. The chance for any given projectile home in is multiplied by the effect chance scalar of the player's current gun. Notes: This item grants stronger homing than Crutch, but does not apply to all bullets.

Blood Brooch is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Increases Curse by 1 while held. Heals half a heart every 1200 damage dealt to enemies. Notes: This item has no effect aside from increasing curse when playing as Robot. The item effect is reminiscent of the rally mechanic in Bloodborne, whereby the player character can regain health by attacking enemies after taking damage. This item and its synergy with the Trick Gun are references to Bloodborne where the old blood refers to the blood of the Great Ones.

+1 Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon, Exit the Gungeon, and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. Effects: Increases damage by 25%. Notes: This item is a reference to common magical items in Dungeons and Dragons, which often show their enhanced nature by being labelled as '+1'. +1 Bullets synergising with Guon Stones is due to +1 Bullets and every Guon Stone being a reference to Dungeons and Dragons. Guon Stones themselves are a reference to Ioun Stones. Fixed Bug Prior to A Farewell To Arms, if Potion of Gun Friendship was dropped while the Friend to Gun and Bullet synergy was active, the orbiting gun would be made permanent.

Backup Gun is a passive item. Effects: Guns simultaneously shoot forwards and backwards. The backwards firing shots will be significantly less accurate. Notes: Gruber's Bane - If player also has Shadow Bullets all backward shots have an additional projectile. Like Shooting Fish - If the player has Barrel, a fish pops out of the Barrel and fires bullets. Interacts strangely with Double Vision - Instead of doubling the backwards shots, a player that activates Double Vision with Backup Gun equipped will fire three shots forwards, an... Trivia: This item is a reference to the movie Die Hard, where the protagonist John McClane tapes his Beretta 92F pistol to his back.

Bouncy Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade. Effects: Allows the player's bullets to bounce off of objects twice. Notes: Absent Minded - If the player also has The Membrane, its shots bounce 4 times and become homing. Bouncy Bullets will also cause tables from Table Tech Rocket to bounce. It works well with Snowballets, as Bouncy Bullets allows them to bounce across the room and grow to massive sizes. Trivia: Bouncy Bullets resemble Rubber Kin while Shock Rounds resemble Tazies.

The Seven-Leaf Clover is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Increases the quality of chests found in chest rooms, mirror rooms, and some secret rooms to a 50-50 chance of red or black. Overrides the usual chance for Magnificence to decrease the quality of chests found in chest rooms. Has no effect on chests found upon clearing rooms and items not found in chests. Notes: Breakfast Club - If the player also has Wind Up Gun, its reload time is halved, fire rate increased by 60%, damage increased by 50%, magazine size increased to 27, and all but the... This item cannot drop from a Rainbow Mode chest Trivia: This item is and contains several references to Futurama.

Sunglasses is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon Effects: Slows down time by 55% and increases dodge roll distance and speed by 25% for 2 seconds after an explosion. This effect has a 10 second cooldown, meaning it cannot be permanently activated by chained explosives. Increases coolness by 2. Notes: This item is a reference to the unflinching walk, a movie cliché popularized by action flicks where a "cool" character (sometimes wearing sunglasses) walks in slow motion as explo... In terms of design, the sunglasses closely resemble those of Horatio Caine, the protagonist of CSI: Miami who is well known for slowly putting on similar shades when announcing a...

Meatbun is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. It also appears as a powerup in Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Heals the player for one heart. Grants double damage until the player is hit. In Exit the Gungeon, the effect will also wear off when the player ascends to the next chamber. Notes: The damage effect is lost if either player is hit in Co-opETG. This effect stacks with itself, meaning that a second meatbun will grant 4x damage, and 3 will grant 8x. Meatbun may be a reference to the California based gaming apparel brand Meat Bun.

Molotov Launcher is a gun in Enter the Gungeon that fires Molotovs, which set large areas on fire. Notes: Unlike the regular Molotov, the Molotov Launcher will work in The Gorgun's arena. Child projectiles created by Flak Bullets will not create puddles of fire. Extra shots fired by Hip Holster will usually not create puddles of fire, however it rarely still does.

Mimic Tooth Necklace is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: All chests (with the exception of Rat chests, and chests that have no corresponding mimic type) are unlocked, and become Mimics. This includes chests spawned before the necklace was picked up. Possible Bug Has an inverse effect on Rat chests, forcing them not to be Mimics. Notes: Has no interaction with Wall Mimics, Door Lord, or the Mimic Gun, despite them being mimics. This item makes Junk significantly more difficult to acquire, as it is acquired from breaking non-mimic chests. Does not affect the chest spawned by Winchester's minigame, rainbow chests, synergy chests, or glitch chests.

Ring of Mimic Friendship is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon Effects: Mimic chests will no longer attack the player, acting like regular chests. Prevents Pedestal Mimics from appearing. Prevents Wall Mimics from awakening. Notes: This item is most valuable to new and inexperienced players, to whom Mimics may pose a serious threat. Does not prevent Door Lord from appearing, despite it technically being a Mimic.

Hot Lead is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Every bullet fired has a 20%† chance to set enemies on Fire (Dur: 4s DPS: 4). Projectiles given this effect are tinted red. † The chance for any given projectile transmogrify in is multiplied by the effect chance scalar of the player's current gun. Fired beams have a ~25% chance per second trained on an enemy to set them on Fire (Dur: 4s DPS: 4). Notes: The Get Equipped with -H- -synergy is a reference to Mega Man II - the Atomic Fire weapon is gained from defeating Heat Man, and represented on the weapon select screen with an H

Yari Launcher is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Rapidly fires tightly homing (Rad: Vel: 220) rockets that create small explosions upon hitting an enemy. The damage dealt by these rockets (both their impact damage and explosive damage) bypass the Boss DPS cap, allowing this gun to kill bosses extremely quickly. Notes: The gun is a reference to the rocket launcher in the 2013 reboot of Shadow Warrior. The orbiting Demon Head gained through the Shadow Warrior synergy is likely a reference to one of the game's weapons, a demon head that shoots a laser out of its mouth.

Shock Rounds is a passive item and a bullet upgrade. Effects: Creates chains of damaging electricity between fired bullets. Doubles the damage of Beam style Guns. Notes: Alistair's Ladder - If the player has Thunderclap, it fires two parallel balls of lightning with every shot, connecting the bullets in a ladder pattern. Battery Powered - If the player has Shock Rifle, the gun's appearance changes and it automatically regenerates ammo over time. Chain Lightning - If the player has Shadow Bullets, the range that Shock Bullets can connect bullets is increased, and lightning can connect to enemy bullets.

Super Space Turtle is a passive item that appeas in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Summons Super Space Turtle to the players side to aid them in combat. ETG Super Space Turtle will run towards enemies, firing bullets that deal 5 damage each. XTG Super Space Turtle will fly towards enemies with the aid of a jetpack, firing at them. Notes: The turtle's bullets are affected by bullet modifiers such as Cursed Bullets and Helix Bullets. Shock Rounds will create a link of electricity between the turtle's bullets and the player's bullets. The turtle will not attack a Mimic without the player first interacting with the Mimic.

Dog is a passive item in Enter and Exit the Gungeon. The Hunter starts with this item in both games. Notes: Cannot be dropped when playing as The Hunter. The dog cannot be interacted with if the player is currently in combat. If the player has flight, they can still interact with the dog but there will be no petting animation.

Super Space Turtle is a passive item that appeas in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Summons Super Space Turtle to the players side to aid them in combat. ETG Super Space Turtle will run towards enemies, firing bullets that deal 5 damage each. XTG Super Space Turtle will fly towards enemies with the aid of a jetpack, firing at them. Notes: The turtle's bullets are affected by bullet modifiers such as Cursed Bullets and Helix Bullets. Shock Rounds will create a link of electricity between the turtle's bullets and the player's bullets. The turtle will not attack a Mimic without the player first interacting with the Mimic.

The Bullet That Can Kill The Past is an important and story-relevant passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Allows a Gungeoneer to access their past with the Gun That Can Kill The Past. Notes: Cannot be dropped. All rooms that are guaranteed to appear due to the required parts will no longer appear after turning the parts in. Obsidian Shell Casing will always drop from High Dragun's skull, but cannot be turned in more than once.
tips.englishDisplayName: Bullet That Can Kill The Past

tips.englishNotes: Explosive Rounds is a passive item and bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Adds an 8.5% chance to fire bullets that explode upon hitting an enemy or object. The explosions deal 25 damage. This effect can only be triggered a maximum of once per second, making it seem like slower fire rate weapons have a higher chance. Notes: The explosions do not harm the player. Explosions can show secret room cracks when using starter guns. The beam damage multiplier sometimes granted by Explosive Rounds does not stack with the beam damage multipliers of Shock Rounds, Flak Bullets, Katana Bullets, Shadow Bullets, Vor... Trivia: Mega Blast might be a reference to Xenon 2 Megablast.

Old Crest is a passive item found in a special room in the Oubliette. Effects: Grants a piece of armor. This piece of armor will always appear at the end of the health bar, and is destroyed upon taking damage. Picking up regular armor before or after the Old Crest will move the Old Crest forward, making it impossible to protect it with armor. Notes: Cannot be dropped. Self-damage from Cigarettes will destroy Old Crest. Cannot be stolen by the Resourceful Rat because it is on a pedestal.

Escape Rope is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Upon use, teleports the player into the Shop, resetting the current room if they are in an uncleared combat room. In the R&G Dept and Bullet Hell, which do not have shops, the player is teleported to the first room of the chamber. Cannot be used in the Resourceful Rat's Lair. Notes: Teleporting out of a boss room will return the boss to full health when the player re-enters. If the player takes damage to a boss and uses the Escape Rope to teleport out, they cannot earn a Master Round even if they take no damage on subsequent attempts at the boss.

Wax Wings is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Grants flight, giving the player the ability to fly over pits and floor traps such as spikes. Flight, by extension, grants immunity to all floor goop, since the player is not actually on the floor to touch it. XTG Holding the Ascending Dodge Roll button will instead slow down time. Notes: Wax Wings utterly trivialise Winchesters shooting gallery, as the player can fly over the pit and shoot every target at point blank range- all but guaranteeing the highest reward. This item is a reference to the Greek legend of Icarus, where the craftsman Daedalus made wings out of wax and feathers to escape Crete with his son Icarus.

Cluster Mine is an active item. Effects: Places 5 small mines that explode when an enemy comes into contact with them, dealing 60 damage. Notes: Cerebral Bros - If the player also has Bullet Bore, the gun shoots an extra drill and turns red. Mine Craft - If the player also has Mine Cutter, mines home in on enemies while Mine Cutter is equipped. Can deal explosive damage to the player.

Cloranthy Ring is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Increases dodge roll speed by 15.8%. Increases the amount of time during a dodge roll that the player is invulnerable. Notes: This item is a reference to a Dark Souls item of the same name. Like in Enter The Gungeon, dodge rolling in Dark Souls is a commonly used move to avoid enemy attacks. In Dark Souls, this ring speeds up stamina recovery. Stamina is used for rolling, as well as attacking and running.

Fairy Wings is an unused passive item. Effects: Grants flight. Rolls leave pink slime that temporarily charms enemies. Notes: Granted by Hungry Caterpillar. Trivia: It is unknown why this item was cut.

Clone is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Upon death, the player respawns at the very beginning of their run with full health, as well as all of their accumulated guns, items, and pickups- with the exception of Clone itse... The Robot will always respawn with 6 Armor. The player always respawns on the chamber they started the original run in. Notes: If the shopkeeper has been angered (ex. shooting in shop, stealing), he will not return after revival. Allows the collection of duplicate Master Rounds on a single run, all of which count towards unlocking the Super Hot Watch.

Blast Helmet is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Decreases the radius at which explosions can harm the player. Increases damage dealt by dodge rolling into enemies by 200% (3x multiplier). For most characters this increases damage dealt per roll from 3 to 9. Notes: Prior to the Advanced Gungeons & Draguns Update, this item did not prevent contact damage or increase dodge roll damage. This item may be a reference to the training helmet that Luke Skywalker wore in Star Wars. The Fightsabre Training synergy spawns an orbital that appears to be a training droid from the same scene.

Monster Blood is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Taking damage spawns a pool of poison goop. Grants immunity to poison. Grants a heart container. Notes: Like all heart container items, The Robot will receive 5 to 10 instead. On the Switch version, and Windows 10 Steam version, the accented characters in the Ammonomicon entry won't show up, instead reading "Mdecins Sans Diplme". This item is a reference to the book Monster Blood, the third title in the original Goosebumps series, written by R. L. Stine.

Nanomachines is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Grants 2 pieces of Armor upon pickup. Grants a piece of Armor every 4 times damage is taken. Damage count does not reset on floor changes. Notes: This item is a reference to the character Senator Steven Armstrong, antagonist and final boss of Metal Gear Rising: Revengence, who uses nanomachines within his body to grant hims... The Ammonomicon quote "Son" in reference to a quote from one of Armstrong's most famous speeches in the game; "Nanomachines, son!". Pretty Good is a reference to the popular recurring quote "You're pretty good", from the Metal Gear franchise.

Gunboots is a passive item. Effects: Dodge rolling fires a spread of five bullets backwards, which deal 8 damage each. Increases movement speed by 1 (approximately 14%). Notes: Gunboots has a cooldown of about 2 seconds between bursts of bullets. Can be used to break targets in Winchester's game. Double Down - If the player also has Springheel Boots, the cooldown is removed and every roll will fire bullets. Trivia: This item, and possibly its method of unlock (found in the elevator by Tailor) are a reference to Downwell.

Gnawed Key is a passive item obtainable only in the shop in Gungeon Proper. It costs a total of 1000 , which can be given over the course of multiple runs. Effects: Unlocks the hatch to the Resourceful Rat's Lair. Notes: The Gnawed Key will not spawn until you have completed the Bullet That Can Kill The Past. The Gnawed Key will not be discounted by discount effects (e.g. Smiley's Revolver, Iron Coin). The key will disappear if the shopkeeper is angered.

R2G2 is a passive item and companion in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Summons a robotic companion that follows the player. When an enemy is in range, it prioritizes following the target and periodically fires a rapid burst of 6 bullets at them. R2G2 is capable of flight, and will fly over pits to keep up with the player or track down enemies. Notes: R2G2's bullets are affected by all bullet modifiers. R2G2 will not attack Red-Caped Bullet Kin. This item is a reference to the iconic robot R2-D2 from the Star Wars franchise.

Ticket is an active item. Effects: Upon use, spawns a friendly Gatling Gull that periodically fires spreads of bullets and flies off once the room is cleared. He will die if he sustains enough damage. Notes: Secret Twin - If the player also has Vulcan Cannon, holding Vulcan Cannon while using Ticket will spawn two friendly Gatling Gulls instead of one. Gatling Gull gains the effects of most bullet modifiers. If the Ticket is used a second time in the same room the first Gatling Gull (or first two with Secret Twin), if still alive, will disappear. Trivia: In previous versions of the game, there were files for an item that spawned Gatling Gull as an ally, but couldn't shoot.

The Gold Ammolet is a passive item and an Ammolet in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: When a Blank is triggered, all enemies within a 25 tile radius of its center point will be Stunned (Dur: 1s) and dealt 60 damage. The damage effect of the Gold Ammolet functions by increasing each blank's regular damage radius to 25, and adding 50 damage (to the default 10). Adds 1 to the player's minimum number of Blanks, restored at the start of every floor. Notes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, the Gold Ammolet increased blank damage to 35, did not grant an extra blank and was an item.

The Lodestone Ammolet is a passive item and an Ammolet in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: When a Blank is triggered, all enemies within a 25 tile radius of its center point will be Stunned (Dur: 3s). Increases the knockback of Blanks by 400% (5x multiplier). Adds 1 to the player's minimum number of Blanks, restored at the start of every floor. Notes: If duplicates of this item are somehow obtained, it further increases the knockback of blanks and grants another blank each floor. Bug If a large quantity of duplicates are somehow obtained (e.g.

Angry Bullets is a passive item and bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Bullets that hit enemies will bounce off them into a random direction. Reflected bullets can trigger the same effect. Reflected bullets deal half of the original bullet's damage. Notes: Beam guns will be deflected at a random angle upon hitting an enemy. Most explosive weapons will not explode upon contact with an enemy. An exception to this is Void Core Cannon, whose projectiles will still explode even with Angry Bullets.

Chaos Ammolet is a passive item and an Ammolet in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: When a Blank is triggered, all enemies within a 25 tile radius of its center point will suffer the following effects; Stun (Dur: 1s) 50% chance to be set on Fire (Dur: 4s DPS: 6). Notes: The Ammonomicon quote "What Can Will" is a reference to the phrase "Anything that can go wrong will go wrong.", known colloquially as Murphy's Law. Bug If an enemy is frozen while spawning it will remain invulnerable to damage until it has thawed and been allowed to properly finish its spawn animation.

Number 2 is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. The Cultist starts with this item. This item is exclusive to co-op mode and cannot be found via normal gameplay until it is unlocked. Effects: Increases the player's movement speed by 2 (base movement speed is 7) and damage by 41% when their co-op partner is dead. Cannot be dropped. Notes: Number 2 was not obtainable in single player until A Farewell to Arms. If obtained on any other gungeoneers, it will travel with them into the past. This item isn't required to unlock Finished Gun.

Corsair is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires slow moving bullets that accelerate and bounce around the room. These bullets have a slight homing effect, curving towards enemies. Upon reaching the end of their lifespan, these bullets explode- creating a large red X at the position of the explosion. Notes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, the bullets did not curve. Corsair's bullets cannot be caught by Gunjurers. "Corsair" is a common name for a pirate or a privateer operating on the high seas, especially ones operating along the southern coast of the Mediterranean in the 16th–18th centuri...

Charge Shot is a gun which fires a large laser that pierces enemies and bounces off of walls twice. The laser deals double damage after bouncing. Notes: Barrage Shot - If the player also has Jetpack, charge time is reduced by 25%, reload time is increased by 50%, and shot size is increased. Prior to the A Farewell to Arms update, Charge Shot's magazine size was 1 and its max ammo was 50. Trivia: This weapon is a reference to the game Charge Shot. Charge Shot's glowing green light turns red and blinks when it is out of ammo. If the Charge Shot's clip is refilled with an ammo pack while empty, it will continue to blink red as if it were still out of ammo until fired again.

Zilla Shotgun is a gun that fires a shotgun blast of bullets. It can be charged to fire the entire magazine at once, which will cause it to fire piercing, bouncing bullets. Notes: The Zilla Shotgun is in the SHOTGUN gun class, and as such benefits from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Zilla Shotgun had 100 maximum ammo. Zilla Shotgun can still fire the charged shot if it has only one shot left in the magazine. This will, however, still consume 4 ammo.

The Emperor is a gun that fires a burst of four big green bullets connected by electricity, identical to the effect of Shock Rounds. Notes: Starburst Fire - If the player also has Laser Rifle, firing either The Emperor or Laser Rifle once will cause it to rapidly fire its entire magazine. You can cancel the effect by reloading or dodge-rolling while firing. Trivia: Unused: The Star War - (CustomSynergyType.THE_STAR_WAR) If the player has Void Marshal, it causes an unknown effect. This gun may be a reference to Emperor Palpatine from Star Wars. This is further reinforced by the name of the above unused synergy, and the fact that the Void Marshal is also a reference to Star Wars.

Science Cannon is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a laser beam with a 0.6s charge-up. If this laser beam is focused on the same enemy for over 0.5s, it has a 60%~ chance per second to apply each of the following status effects; Charm (Dur: 6s)

Lil' Bomber is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Charges up to fire bouncing, exploding bombs. Like most explosive weapons, this explosion is capable of destroying some enemy bullets. These bombs are uniquely capable of blasting open secret rooms. Deals 30% extra damage to bosses. Notes: Bomberpal and Super Bomberpal stack, allowing Lil' Bomber to fire three bombs at once if the player has both Bomb and Ice Bomb. The King Bomber synergy can provide up to 420 per ammo pickup, or up to 840 with Loot Bag. With both Bomberpal and Super Bomberpal, assuming all three projectiles hit, this number can be up to 1260 per ammo pickup, or 2520 with Loot Bag.

Mutation is a gun that fires a slightly homing continuous beam with heavy recoil, leaving a trail of red liquid. Mutation also turns enemies red, though this is a purely cosmetic effect. Notes: The synergy between the Mutation and Bionic Leg/Shock Rounds may be a reference to Tetsuo from the 1982 manga series Akira. "Didn't Need Two Anyway" refers to the look of the weapon in-game, which envelops the player's whole arm. This visual effect is also shared with Robot's Right Hand, Robot's Left Hand, Heroine, Flame Hand and Megahand.

Wind Up Gun is a gun that fires purple lasers. The first half of the magazine pierces and deals full damage, and the second half deals 50% damage and does not pierce. Notes: Breakfast Club - If the player also has Seven-Leaf Clover, reload time is halved, fire rate is increased by 60%, damage is increased by 50%, magazine size is increased to 27, and... Wound Up - If the player also has Oiled Cylinder, the bottom half of the magazine retains full damage and piercing, and the damage and bullet size is increased by 25%. With increased magazine size, all shots except for the last ten deal full damage and pierce, and only the last ten shots deal less damage.

Silencer is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires giant zippers. The gun is swung forward when fired. Hitting an enemy with this close-ranged swing will stun the enemy. Trivia: This gun is a reference to the myth of pillows being used as makeshift silencers. 300 Dead Count is a pun on "Thread Count", a term often used to measure the quality of sheets. The character of Alben Smallbore mentioned in the Ammonomicon entry of this and the Flame Hand is a reference to Headmaster Albus Dumbledore from the Harry Potter series.

Pitchfork is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. Effects: Launches fireballs that set enemies on Fire (Dur: 4s DPS: 4). ETG Each fireball can pierce through up to 4 enemies. Notes: Fallen Bullet Kin wield the Pitchfork. CMV Possible Bug The special UI icon for both the Pitchfork and Pitch Perfect in the Chinese Mobile Version of Enter the Gungeon appear blue.

Composite Gun is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: The Blank effect of this gun can open the entrance to secret rooms. When checking for secret rooms in the Shop, a charged bullet can be fired into the shop from an adjacent room to avoid angering Bello. The charged shot of this gun does not trigger the Blank Shrine.

Gunther is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It is alive, talks, and grows stronger with each room cleared. Effects: Gunther has 3 stages of behavior. Upon clearing a room, there is a chance to gain friendship with Gunther, indicated by him talking. First Stage: Fires piercing bullets at 6 damage. Second Stage: Besides piercing, bullets deal 9 damage and bounce twice. Notes: Similar to other guns with infinite ammo, Gunther cannot be combined with other guns using Duct Tape, be fed to the muncher, or reveal secret rooms. Gunjurers cannot absorb Gunther's bullets at stage 3. The bullets of this gun ignore the Unfriendly Fire Challenge Mode modifier.

Mahoguny is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a single explosive pinecone along with three drifting leaf bullets. Notes: The explosions created by the pinecones can destroy enemy bullets. Mahoguny is a portmanteau of gun and mahogany.

Lower Case r is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a burst of 6 letter-shaped projectiles that spell out the word BULLET. While firing, the gun will voice out the word "bullet". Notes: The Lower Case r is voiced by Doseone, the composer of Enter the Gungeons soundtrack. This gun, while obviously a surface level reference to the basic shape of the letter r, may also be a reference to early computer adventure games. The reload animation of the Lower Case r consists of a text cursor deleting the letter with a backspace, and typing an entirely new r.

Buzzkill is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires sawblades that can ricochet off walls 3 times, and pierce through 2 enemies each. Notes: Buzzkill is slang for someone who ruins fun (in this case, the gun's designer from the Ammonomicon entry), and the gun's name is also a portmanteau of buzzsaw and kill. The pickup text "Sawed On!" is a play on words, based on a "sawed-off shotgun." May be a reference to the Disc Gun in the game Nuclear Throne which also fires ricocheting saw blades.

Uranium Ammolet is a passive item and an Ammolet in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: When a Blank is triggered, all enemies within a 25 tile radius of its center point will be Stunned (Dur: 1s) and have a 50% chance to be Poisoned (Dur: 6s DPS: 3). Adds 1 to the player's minimum number of Blanks, restored at the start of every floor. Notes: The item's description is a reference to Marie Curie, a Polish physicist who conducted pioneering research on radioactivity, but died due to aplastic anemia, caused by the high le...

Copper Ammolet is a passive item and an Ammolet in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: When a Blank is triggered, all enemies within a 25 tile radius of its center point will be Stunned (Dur: 1s) and have a 50% chance to be set on Fire (Dur: 4s DPS: 6). Adds 1 to the player's minimum number of Blanks, restored at the start of every floor. Notes: Bug If a large quantity of duplicates are somehow obtained (e.g.

Frost Ammolet is a passive item and an Ammolet in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: When a Blank is triggered, all enemies within a 25 tile radius of its center point will be Stunned (Dur: 1s) and have a 50% chance to be Frozen (Amount: 100%). Adds 1 to the player's minimum number of Blanks, restored at the start of every floor. Notes: The synergy Don’t Worry About The Vase is a reference to The Matrix. The synergy Arctic Warfare - is most likely a reference to the A.W.P. Asiimov skin from Counter Strike.

Fightsabre is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Automatic weapon with a consistent firerate. While reloading, the weapon is swung around and reflects nearby enemy bullets back towards their owners. Reflected bullets deal 10 damage each. Notes: The reflect timing (and animation) is longer than the audio cue would suggest. The Fightsabre can deflect the High Dragun's rocket attack. The Fightsabre cannot deflect enemy lasers.

Huntsman is a gun that fires shotgun blasts of 6 bullets. Reloading swings the axe, destroying bullets and knocking back enemies in a short range. Curse is increased by 1 while this gun is held. Notes: Peculiarly, the Huntsman is a part of the FULLAUTO gun class despite not being remotely fully automatic. Because the Huntsman is not SHOTGUN type despite being a shotgun, it does not benefit from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. Trivia: The fact that the weapon increases Curse while being carried stems from the Gungeon rejecting non-bullet based weapons. Executioners wield this weapon. The Huntsman appears to be a Regular Shotgun with an axe blade attached to the underside of the barrel.

Shotgrub is a gun that fires a spread of 5 bullets that leave small pools of poison goop on impact. Notes: Contrail - If the player has Alien Engine, its flame turns green, igniting enemies with Green Fire (Dur: 120s DPS: 4). Alien Engine also grants flight while held. Just Like The Real Thing - If the player has Sixth Chamber, Yellow Chamber, Holey Grail, or Bullet Idol, Shotgrub's bullets will act like those of the Shotgrub enemy, no longer ca... Monster Grub - If the player also has Monster Blood, Shotgrub's bullets will home in on enemies. Trivia: "No Worries" is a reference to the song Hakuna Matata, which is sung by Timon and Pumba from the movie The Lion King, who are notorious for eating grubs.

Prime Primer is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. It can be purchased for 110 in a shop in the Gungeon Proper. Effects: One of four components of the Bullet That Can Kill The Past. Notes: This item is useless on The Cultist as it cannot be dropped and the Blacksmith will refuse to talk with the second player. As it is in a glass display case, it can't be stolen with the Grappling Hook or the Directional Pad's grappling hook ability. Surviving Bello's onslaught of bullets in an attempt to steal it will close the shop and the Primer will disappear.

Planar Lead is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: One of four components of the Bullet That Can Kill The Past. Notes: The item cannot be dropped, so if The Cultist picks up the item, there is no way for it to be given to the Blacksmith. A hidden (possibly bugged) text string that may appear in the Diginomocon in Exit the Gungeon describes the Planar Lead as being bestowed upon a telepathic bullet who would lead t... After giving the Planar Lead to the Blacksmith, it will appear on the Hero of Time Statue in the Breach as a helmet.

Obsidian Shell Casing is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: One of four components of the Bullet That Can Kill The Past. Cannot be dropped, and will not be stolen by the Resourceful Rat. Notes: This is the only bullet component that can be re-acquired after giving it to the Blacksmith. After giving the Obsidian Shell Casing to the Blacksmith, it will appear on the Hero of Time Statue in the Breach as armour.

Arcane Gunpowder is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Cannot be used. One of four components of the Bullet That Can Kill The Past. Notes: Bug If either player in a co-op game dies and is holding the gunpowder, the Arcane Gunpowder will disappear. If the Cultist is holding the gunpowder, it’s not possible to give the gunpowder to the Blacksmith, as the gunpowder is not droppable. Cannot be stolen by the Resourceful Rat.

Shadow Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade obtainable in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Firing a weapon has a 15% chance to fire an additional projectile. Gives beam style Guns a 15% chance every second while firing to gain double damage for one second, creating random seconds of double damage. Notes: The beam damage multiplier sometimes granted by Shadow Bullets does not stack with the beam damage multipliers of Shock Rounds, Flak Bullets, Explosive Rounds, Katana Bullets, Vor... While the damage multiplier itself does not stack, the chance for the multiplier to be applied per-second does.

Enraging Photo is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. The Convict starts with this item in both games. Effects: Upon taking damage, all of the player's guns will be instantly reloaded, and the player will be enraged for 4 seconds, dealing double damage. Notes: Cannot be dropped when playing as The Convict. This item will carry over into characters' pasts. When found as other Gungeoneers, it cannot be sold to the Sell Creep or eaten by the serpent.

Military Training is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. The Marine starts with this item in both games. Effects: Decreases reload times by 20%. Reduces weapon spread by 17%, increasing accuracy. Decreases charge times for charged guns by 16.7%. Notes: Cannot be dropped when playing as The Marine. Carries over to the pasts. This is the only item that affects guns' charge time. However, there are a number of synergies that reduce the charge time of one of the guns involved in them.

Chromesteel Assault Rifle is an unobtainable gun in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: Hegemony soldiers in The Convict's past are equipped with this gun. The Black Stache's boss-card depicts several Hegemony Soldiers holding the rifle. Due to the way enemy AI is coded in Enter the Gungeon, companions and enemies which use weapons disconnected from their sprite hold real guns which are theoretically usable in the... However, the fact that the Chromesteel Assault Rifle has a unique description in the Ammonomicon suggests that it may have at one point been intended for player use.

Trusty Lockpicks is an active item in Enter the Gungeon. The Pilot starts with this item. Effects: Has a 50% chance to unlock a lock or locked chest. If it fails, the lock will no longer function.
