Winchester is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. It is similar to the Regular Shotgun, and has a higher fire rate, lower reload time, and less spread, but less max ammo. Notes: The Winchester is wielded by Blue Shotgun Kin. The quote "Better Than A Box Of Roses" is a reference to a scene in the movie Terminator 2: Judgement Day, where the Terminator pulls a Winchester out of a box full of roses. When reloading the Winchester, the player will spin the weapon backwards around the trigger hand. This is in imitation of what the Terminator does in the movie.

The Rogue Special is a Gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It is the starting weapon of The Pilot. Notes: Cannot be dropped. Cannot be obtained by any Gungeoneer other than The Pilot or The Paradox. While reloading, the gun emits three jets of fog in the shape of a radiation symbol.

The Budget Revolver is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It serves as The Convict's starter gun in both games. Notes: Cannot be dropped. Cannot be obtained by any Gungeoneer other than The Convict or The Paradox. The gun appears to be modeled after a variety of snub-nosed revolvers.

Hungry Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade. Effects: Adds a chance for the player's bullets to become blue. Blue projectiles will 'eat' any enemy projectiles within a radius of 1.5 tiles around them, destroying them. For each bullet 'eaten', the projectile will gain a 10% damage and size increase. Individual hungry bullets will become 'sated' after eating 8 projectiles, and will no longer consume any more, effectively capping the damage and size multiplier per bullet at 80%. Notes: Hungry Bullets is usually more effective for guns that fire many bullets in a large spread, such as shotguns. Does nothing against enemy beams, such as those fired by the Shelleton, Beholster, or Det.

Kruller Glaive is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Charges up to throw a glaive projectile. This glaive travels in an arc, returning to the player like a boomerang. This glaive can come in on enemies while it travels, deviating from its arc. Enemies struck by the glaive are stunned for 5 seconds. Notes: The Kruller Glaive is one of the three weapons in the NONE gun class that can be obtained in normal play, the others being the Chamber Gun and the Slinger. If the player has Helix Bullets, glaives lose their homing. Due to the weapon's movement pattern, Gunjurers can't reclaim the projectile to throw it back at the player, making it useful to deal with them.

Flash Ray is a gun that fires piercing beams that have a chance to stun enemies. Notes: Ice Cap - If the player also has Freeze Ray, it freezes enemies much quicker. Savior of the Universe - If the player also has Camera, Flash Ray gains double magazine size, unlimited piercing, a thicker beam, one bounce, increased fire rate, and causes a Cam... Whiter Guon Stone - If the player also has White Guon Stone, the size of the Guon Stone is increased, it rotates at a fixed distance from the player while they are moving instead... Trivia: Everything about this gun is a reference to Flash Gordon. The quote "Ah Ahhh!" is a reference to Queen's song Flash, which is Flash Gordon's theme song.

Proton Backpack is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires two slow-moving simultaneous beams; A thick homing (Rad: 18 Vel: 420) red laser that deals very low damage. A thin electric homing (Rad: 15 Vel: 420) beam that deals the majority of the Proton Backpack's DPS. Notes: This gun is a reference to the iconic Proton Pack ghost fighting weapon from the Ghostbusters franchise. The Ammonomicon quote "Crossing Streams" is in reference to the repeated warning in Ghostbusters (1984) "Don't cross the streams", as crossing the streams from multiple Proton Bac...

The Exotic is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires an explosive missile that can pierce up to 10 enemies. While held, grants a small robotic companion that blocks bullets and reveals the contents of nearby chests. If the player has revealed the contents of a chest, the contents of that chest will be permanently locked in as that item. Notes: The Exotic is a reference to the exotic-rarity rocket launcher Gjallarhorn from Destiny, one of the most beloved and memetic exotics in the game. The robot companion is a reference to the Ghosts from Destiny.

Regular Shotgun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon, Exit the Gungeon, and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. Notes: The Regular Shotgun has an equip animation in which the gungeoneer racks the pump. ETG The Regular Shotgun is in the SHOTGUN gun class, meaning it benefits from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. The Regular Shotgun is the gun wielded by Red Shotgun Kin, Ashen Shotgun Kin, and Mutant Shotgun Kin.

Orbital Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: The players bullets, after hitting a wall or obstacle, will instantly return to the player and begin to orbit around them for 15 seconds. Bullets orbit at a random distance from the player, between 2 and 5 tiles. Orbiting bullets no longer collide with walls, but can still collide with enemies and other world objects like doors and chests. Notes: Orbiting bullets can still destroy chests. Care should be taken when entering chest rooms if any orbiting bullets are still present. Orbiting bullets do not reveal secret rooms, even if they came from a weapon that could.

Partially-Eaten Cheese is an active item that can be found in one of the four chests after defeating the Resourceful Rat. Effects: While active, the player turns into Pac-Man, becoming invincible, granting flight, and instantly killing any enemies they come in contact with. This item can eat enemies that are usually invulnerable: this includes the Gunreaper, Lead Cube and Flesh Cube. Bosses take 30 damage instead of dying instantly, and the player will bounce off them. Notes: Resourceful Indeed - If the player also has Rat Boots, Elimentaler, and Resourceful Sack, the player becomes the Resourceful Rat, preventing items from being stolen when they're l...

Resourceful Sack is an active item that can be found in one of the four chests after defeating the Resourceful Rat. Effects: Rolling through bullets collects them in the sack. This effect works even if the Resourceful Sack is not the currently selected item. The sack holds a maximum of 30 shots，each shot deals 5×2 damage. Additional bullets rolled through will disappear without adding to the total. Notes: Resourceful Indeed - If the player also has Partially-Eaten Cheese, Elimentaler, and Rat Boots, the player becomes the Resourceful Rat, preventing items from being stolen when the...

Baby Good Mimic is a companion passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Follows the player around. When in a room with enemies, Baby Good Mimic will sit still and pretend to be a chest. Enemies that touch Baby Good Mimic directly will take contact damage. If the player has other companions, Baby Good Mimic will permanently transform itself into a second copy of the oldest companion the player has. Notes: Finishing a run or causing a chest to explode with Baby Good Mimic unlocks Baby Good Shelleton. While hiding in its base form, player-owned explosions can trigger Baby Good Mimic to begin attacking.

Macho Brace is a passive item that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: After dodge rolling, the player briefly gains a yellow aura for 0.4 seconds. The first shot fired with this aura will break it, but deal 30% more damage. If the player shoots bullets during their dodge roll (either using an item or by firing before coming to a complete stop) they will break the aura prematurely, but will not gain a... Possible Bug If the player rolls again immediately upon regaining control after a dodge roll, the damage increase from macho brace will not be allowed to activate. Notes: When playing as The Paradox, the glow around the player appears pink and white.

Table Tech Heat is a table tech passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Flipping a table creates a burning aura with a 5 tile radius around the table, which lasts for 10 seconds. All enemies caught in this aura are set on Fire (Dur: 4s DPS: 4). Notes: "Tabla" is Hindi for "Table", while a sutra is a kind of short Indian literary composition intended to teach rituals, literature, philosophy, law, or a great number of other conce... Despite the other Table Techs being included in the Paperwork synergy with Origuni, Table Tech Shotgun and Table Tech Heat are not part of the synergy. CMV Bug The heat ring created by Table Tech Heat is incorrectly coloured blue.

Rat Boots is a passive item that can be found in one of the four chests after defeating the Resourceful Rat. Effects: Grants temporary invulnerability and flight when the wearer walks out over a pit for about 1 second. Increases movement speed by 1.5. Notes: Resourceful Indeed - If the player also has Partially-Eaten Cheese, Elimentaler, and Resourceful Sack, the player becomes the Resourceful Rat, preventing items from being stolen w... Use of Cigarettes is not possible during the invincibility period. Can be used to cheat in some of Winchester's games by walking over the gaps and onto solid ground inside the game area.

The Detective, also known as Lamey is a Gungeoneer exclusive to Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead, where she serves as one of two playable characters alongside Super Space Turtle. Notes: The Detective has a handful of design changes between her unused appearance as Lamey in Enter the Gungeon and her first canon appearance in House of the Gundead. Her ETG appearance also wears lipstick absent from the HoTG design. ETG Bug Attempting to enter Boss Rush as Lamey locks up the game, preventing movement inputs until the game is restarted.

High Dragunfire is a gun that fires purple bullets resembling flames that have a 1/9 chance to burn enemies. Notes: The secret room containing the High Dragunfire contains six friendly cultists kneeling to a red chest, with various dialogue: First time: "Welcome, fellow acolyte! It appears that Gun Cult fashion has changed substantially since our forefathers were sealed into this holy chamber. After reveal: "Our holy relic... a simple gun that cannot kill the past? Trivia: The High Dragunfire and the cultist's dialogue is a reference to old screenshots of the game, featuring a pink Dragunfire and smaller bullets. The shape of the gun is remarkably similar to that of a Bolter from Warhammer 40,000.

Gamma Ray is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a green laser beam that slightly curves towards enemies. Continually shooting an enemy with the laser for 0.25 seconds will poison them for 3.5 seconds. Bug This beam is capable of extinguishing the fireplace in the Keep of The Lead Lord, despite not leaving any goop in its base form. Notes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Gamma Ray did not curve towards enemies. The Ammonomicon quote "Mean Green" may be a reference to The Hulk, who was transformed by exposure to gamma radiation. The Beta Ray synergy is likely a reference to beta decay particles.

Elephant Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: ETG Fires a conical spread of bullets with strong knockback. Notes: So called elephant guns were large guns designed for the hunting of large animals (such as elephants) during safaris in Africa in the 19th century. The Ammonomicon description "These guns were once used to make pianos out of teeth." is a reference to the practice of hunting elephants to harvest their tusks for ivory, a materi... The Ammonomicon description "An inelegant weapon for a less civilized age" is a reference to the iconic line "This is the weapon of a Jedi Knight.

Machine Pistol is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: This gun resembles a Mini Uzi, a compact, blowback-operated machine pistol developed in 1980, chambered in 9x19mm Parabellum. This gun is used by Bandana Bullet Kin, and is one of the six guns wielded by the Beholster. As it is held by the Beholster, it can be placed on the Beholster Shrine.

Pea Shooter is a Gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It shoots low-damage peas. Notes: A peashooter is a type of toy blowgun. The term "pea shooter" is also used derogatorily to describe a weak, small-caliber firearm. The Pea Shooter is given to you by Ser Manuel in the tutorial. The Pea Shooter may be a reference to the Plants Vs.

Dueling Pistol is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires large, piercing musket balls which bounce twice. Notes: The Hell Singing synergy is likely a reference to Van Helsing, the vampire hunter created by Bram Stoker. The Dead Prez synergy is likely a reference to the death of Abraham Lincoln, who was shot with a Derringer, and the Dueling Pistol likely is referencing the many duels that Andrew... The entry for the Dueling Pistol in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of one musketball gundead shooting another dead with the weapon.

Laser Rifle is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It fires a burst of three instantaneous purple laser bolts. Notes: The weapon is likely a reference to the Laser Rifle from X-COM: UFO Defense, which it strongly resembles in both appearance and function. It may also be a reference to the more recent X-COM games, which also sport similar looking laser weapons. If the player also has Vorpal Bullets while they have the Starburst Fire synergy, it is possible for Vorpal Bullets to trigger multiple times in a row, and will deal massive damag...

Dragunfire is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires piercing flame bullets that have a 1/9 chance to burn enemies. Notes: The design of the Dragunfire is that of the Bolter from the dystopian science-fantasy universe of Warhammer 40,000. The name of the gun comes from the Dragonfire round which the Bolter can fire. The tagline "Roar" is a reference to the Bolter's unique roaring sound when fired.

Blunderbuss is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It charges up to fire a short ranged shotgun blast of bouncing bullets. Notes: Despite firing like a shotgun, the Blunderbuss is not in the SHOTGUN gun class, and as such does not benefit from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. There is a glitch where firing Blunderbuss by charging until just before the fully charged animation causes the gun to fire without using ammo. The description in the Ammonomicon is a reference to Farmer Giles of Ham by J.R.R. Tolkien.

Snowballer is a gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It fires snowballs that slowly freeze enemies. Notes: The snowballs leave behind pools of water, which can be used to put out chest fuses and the fireplace in the Keep of the Lead Lord. The Frosty the Bullet Tyrant synergy may be a reference to the boss Bleak from Donkey Kong Country 3, a snowman that throws snowballs that appear to increase in size as they trave... Notably, Bleak also shares a similar appearance to Frosty The Snowman.

Excaliber is a gun that fires a burst of three piercing sword projectiles. Reloading swings the sword, destroying nearby bullets and damaging nearby enemies. Increases curse by 2.5 while held. Notes: Buster Gun - If the player has RPG, Excaliber fires three or four shots in a cone whenever it is reloaded. Snicker-snack - If the player has Vorpal Bullets, Excaliber fires a spread of 3 homing shots that deal 20% less damage. Throw All Daggers - If the player has Knife Shield, it generates two layers of knives upon use. Trivia: Unused: - (CustomSynergyType.SHARDBLADE) If the player has Sprun, it will give Sprun a secondary trigger.

38 Special is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: Spamming the fire button while dodge rolling causes the gun to fire considerably faster for a brief time after the dodge roll has concluded. With a fire rate of 0.07, the 38 Special requires a minimum of ~14 clicks per second to fire at maximum DPS, meaning the player is likely to be unable to take full advantage of th... This can be mitigated on PC by temporarily binding the player's fire keys to ScrollUp and ScrollDown on the scroll wheel.

Plague Pistol is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Bullets fired from the Plague Pistol have a 50% chance to inflict the poison effect on enemies. These bullets also leave a small pool of lingering poison goop on the ground when destroyed. Notes: Because bullets from the Plague Pistol leave non-flammable goop on the ground, they can be used to extinguish chest fuses and the fireplace. The entry for the Plague Pistol in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of a Mutant Bullet Kin.

Thunderclap is a gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Quickly fires small balls of electricity. ETG Any pools of water that these balls pass over will be electrified, dealing considerable damage to enemies. This gun does not grant electricity immunity. As such, electrified pools of water also pose considerable danger to the player. Notes: Altered: Diazepam - Prior to the Advanced Gungeons & Draguns Update, this gun was included in Diazepam. The damaging field while reloading was added in A Farewell to Arms. This weapon's appearance is a reference to the Thunderbolt weapon from the video game series Quake and the Plasma Gun from Doom.

M1 is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. In the Apple Arcade version of Exit the Gungeon, the M1 is called the Grand Rifle. Effects: Bullets from the M1 pierce through enemies and decorations, and travel at an extremely high speed. On reload, the M1 makes a distinctive "ping" sound. Notes: Although classified as semi-automatic, the M1 is functionally automatic, as its rate of fire does not benefit from tapping the fire button instead of holding it down. There are several real guns with the name 'M1', however the shape (such as the band around the rifle's forend), distinctive pinging reload sound, and Ammonomicon entry of the weap...

Thompson Sub-Machinegun is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: ETG The Thompson Sub-Machinegun is wielded by Hollowpoints. "Myeah, See!" references a line spoken by Edward G. Robinson's gangster character in the 1931 film Little Caesar. This gun is used in The Convict's past.

Corsair is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires slow moving bullets that accelerate and bounce around the room. These bullets have a slight homing effect, curving towards enemies. Upon reaching the end of their lifespan, these bullets explode- creating a large red X at the position of the explosion. Notes: Prior to the Supply Drop Update, the bullets did not curve. Corsair's bullets cannot be caught by Gunjurers. "Corsair" is a common name for a pirate or a privateer operating on the high seas, especially ones operating along the southern coast of the Mediterranean in the 16th–18th centuri...

Crestfaller is a gun that fires bullets which freeze enemies. Notes: Crestfallen Soul - If the player also has Gun Soul, dodge roll speed is increased by 20% and Crestfaller deals 50% more damage if the player dies and has not yet retrieved their s... Five O'Clock Somewhere - If the player also has Shotga Cola, Shotgun Coffee, Double Vision, Potion of Gun Friendship, Potion of Lead Skin, or Old Knight's Flask, reloading will fi... As with Gungine, pressing the reload button while the clip is full will still clear liquids. Trivia: Altered: Diazepam - Prior to the Advanced Gungeons & Draguns Update, this gun was included in Diazepam.

Banana is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Flings a banana that bounces once, and explodes upon destruction dealing 30 damage to nearby enemies. Each time this banana hits a wall, regardless of whether it bounces or explodes, it creates two more bananas. The banana will also release two sub-bananas if it reaches the end of its range and detonates mid-air, or if it impacts an enemy. Notes: This gun likely is a reference to the game Gorillas, itself referenced with a weapon of the same name from Worms. The pickup quote is a reference to the plantain, a banana cultivar subgroup.

Abyssal Tentacle is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It fires a tentacle which homes in on and grips enemies, ignoring obstacles. Notes: The tentacle can grip enemies through walls. The tentacle can damage disappearing bosses like Cannonbalrog and High Priest if they are connected and kept while vanished. This gun is a reference to the character "Purple Tentacle" — a sentient, disembodied tentacle with plans for world domination — from the LucasArts game Day of the Tentacle.

Klobbe is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It rapidly fires inaccurate, low-damage bullets. Notes: This gun is among those Gunsling King and Manservantes can challenge the player to clear a room with. This gun is a reference to the Klobb from GoldenEye 007 (itself based on the real-life Škorpion), a notoriously bad gun with similar weaknesses as the Klobbe. The Klobbering Time synergy is a reference to Fantastic Four member The Thing, whose catchphrase is "It's clobbering time".

Molotov Launcher is a gun in Enter the Gungeon that fires Molotovs, which set large areas on fire. Notes: Unlike the regular Molotov, the Molotov Launcher will work in The Gorgun's arena. Child projectiles created by Flak Bullets will not create puddles of fire. Extra shots fired by Hip Holster will usually not create puddles of fire, however it rarely still does.

Nail Gun is a gun in Enter the Gungeon that fires a rapid stream of low damage nails. Notes: This gun is among those Gunsling King and Manservantes can challenge the player to clear a room with. The Ammonomicon entry is a reference to the 1990s horror film Arachnophobia. This weapon may also be a reference to Home Improvement: Power Tool Pursuit!, as the character in that game can also wield a nail gun, which shares the inaccuracy, fire rate, and...

Gunbow is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires fast-moving bullets that bounce seven times. Notes: Enemies killed by the gunbow are vaporised, and leave no corpse behind. The name of this gun could be a reference to the Monster Hunter series, which features Bowguns.

Big Iron is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It fires a shotgun spread of 3ETG/2XTG bullets. Notes: ETG Despite Big Iron using bullets in its ammo bar, shell cartridges come out of its compartment when reloading. ETG Despite firing a spread of multiple bullets like a shotgun, the Big Iron is not in the SHOTGUN gun class, instead residing in the PISTOL class. This gun is a reference to the Marty Robbins song of the same name.

Hyper Light Blaster is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires pink lasers, with high damage per shot. Has an extremely low maximum ammo, however landing a shot on an enemy will restore 1 ammo to the gun. Notes: Shot creatures do not need to be hostile to restore ammo to this gun. As such, shooting chickens and rats will still restore ammunition. Shooting an enemy that is in the midst of its death animation will still restore ammo, as long as the enemy hitbox still blocks bullets. Other shootable targets like chests, cursed pots, or explosive barrels will not restore ammo to the Hyper Light Blaster.

Alien Sidearm is a gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Its behaviour is starkly different in each game. Notes: This gun is a reference to the alien pistols usable in Halo. Shield Breaker is a reference to Halo's famous shield mechanic. The charging mechanic is also a function of Halo's plasma pistol. A fully charged shot from that gun could instantly break another player's shields in multiplayer.

Shock Rifle is a gun that fires a zap of electricity. It can electrify water, and grants immunity to electricity while held. Notes: Assault and Battery - If the player also has the M16, the guns are dual wielded. Battery Powered - If the player has R2G2 or Shock Rounds, Shock Rifle gains a smartphone battery-esque look and automatically regenerates 1 ammo every 4 seconds. When fired at a Gunjurer, the bullets are fired back at a slower speed. Instead of being fired back as a laser-like bullet, it now looks like a thunder bolt. Trivia: Removed: - If the player has Battery Bullets, shot speed is doubled. Likely removed because it includes a starting item.

Flame Hand is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Notes: As the Flame Hand deals fire-type damage, it deals no damage to Muzzle Wisps and Muzzle Flares. ETG Due to the high knockback and large size of the fireballs, they can hit the same enemy multiple times. 2D6 + Int mod is a reference to Dungeons and Dragons

Hegemony Rifle is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires blue energy bolts with considerable spread. Notes: This gun appears to be based on the DH-17 Carbine from Star Wars. The comment about accuracy likely references the historically bad aim of Stormtroopers in Star Wars. However, Stormtroopers never used the DH-17. The weapon's sprites refer to it as "E12", which is a reference to the E-11 Carbine which is the actual standard-issue weapon of Stormtroopers.

Cactus is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It rapidly fires low-damage needles. Notes: "1000 Needles!" is a reference to a move used by the Cactuar, a recurring enemy from the Final Fantasy series, who resembles a walking cactus. "Succulent DOOM" may possibly be a reference to the album Special Herbs by MF DOOM. The "undiscovered Chamber" in the Cactus' description may be the unused Old West floor.

Luxin Cannon is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon which rapidly fires small, solidified beams of light at enemies. When the magazine is emptied, the tip of the crystal will be launched. Notes: This gun is a reference to The Lightbringer book series by Brent Weeks. "Falsely rumored to have natural healing abilities" is a reference to crystal healing, a pseudoscientific alternative medicine technique that employs stones and crystals. The The Return synergy is a reference to Ganon from the Legend of Zelda video game series.

Face Melter is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Simultaneously fires four streams of musical notes at 90° intervals. Creates an amplifier on the ground when reloading. While firing, the active amplifier will fire bullets of its own in sync with the main gun, including aim angle. Only one amplifier can be active at a time. Creating a new amplifier by reloading will remove any previous ones. Notes: Amplifiers do not have hitboxes, meaning that enemies can potentially stand on top of them and be damaged by all four note streams at once. Possible Bug In co-op, if playing as The Cultist, reloading will drop the amplifier at the other player's location.

Bee Hive is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires bees that home (Rad: 10 Vel: 150) in on and damage enemies. The initial 3 damage impact of each bee is affected by the players damage stat, but the lingering swarm damage is not. Notes: ETG If the player feeds the Bee Hive and the RPG to a regular Muncher, it will always spit out a Stinger. ETG Bees will not home in on enemies that are not necessary for room clear. ETG Bees caught by Gunjurers retain their quick movement and tight homing, making them extrmely hard for the player to dodge.

Trashcannon is a gun that fires a trash bag, which explodes into 6 poison projectiles flying 60° apart from each other that create pools of poison. Notes: Garbage Collecting - If the player has Unity, hitting an enemy with Trashcannon causes it and its bullets to briefly freeze. Grouch - Junk increases the damage of the Trashcannon by 25%. This effect does not stack with multiple Junk (despite the Ammonomicon entry claiming otherwise). Recycling Bin - Starpew transforms the Trashcannon into a recycling bin, causing it to shoot high damage recycled shells instead of trash, and also greatly increasing its magazine...

Flash Ray is a gun that fires piercing beams that have a chance to stun enemies. Notes: Ice Cap - If the player also has Freeze Ray, it freezes enemies much quicker. Savior of the Universe - If the player also has Camera, Flash Ray gains double magazine size, unlimited piercing, a thicker beam, one bounce, increased fire rate, and causes a Cam... Whiter Guon Stone - If the player also has White Guon Stone, the size of the Guon Stone is increased, it rotates at a fixed distance from the player while they are moving instead... Trivia: Everything about this gun is a reference to Flash Gordon. The quote "Ah Ahhh!" is a reference to Queen's song Flash, which is Flash Gordon's theme song.

Flare Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires flares which set enemies on Fire (Dur: 4s DPS: 4). Notes: After hitting a wall, fired flares will continue burning for a time. These lingering flares produce light, but have no combat utility. The Synergy with Medkit may be a reference to how flare guns and medical supplies are often kept together for emergency situations. The quote “Over Here” may be a reference to the original Jurassic Park film.

The Vulcan Cannon is a gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. In the Apple Arcade version of Exit the Gungeon, the Vulcan Cannon is called the Gull Cannon. Notes: According to its ammonomicon entry, the Vulcan Cannon is wielded by the Gatling Gull, however its visual appearance is starkly different to the minigun the Gatling Gull can be see... The gun is a reference to a common trope of action films where people will carry handheld M134 Miniguns, which in reality is very difficult and impractical. The Not Quite As Mini synergy is a reference to the Minigun from Team Fortress 2 in appereance and function and subsequently reaffirms this.

Helix is a gun that fires two waving bullets. Notes: Double Double Helix - If the player also has Helix Bullets, instead of the Helix Bullets effect being applied to each bullet, Helix's projectiles will be doubled (2 bullets will f... The Helix is a surprisingly powerful weapon, though difficult to use against regular enemies due to the way it fires. If the player also has Remote Bullets, the spread of the two bullets is reduced as they follow the crosshair, making it easier to use on smaller enemies. Trivia: The gun likely refers to DNA, which has a double helix shape that is similar to the bullets' path. The phrase "Splice n' Dice" is a reference to gene splicing.

Barrel is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires blue fish. ETG Fish have a 20% chance to Stun (Dur: 2.5s) enemies, and leave pools of water when flopping onto the ground that are capable of extinguishing chest fuses and the fireplace. Notes: The gun itself as well as its flavor text are a reference to the idiom "Like shooting fish in a barrel" describing an effortless or simple action, with guaranteed success. One Fish Two Fish Is a clear reference to a book of a similar name written by Dr. Seuss. This gun's quote in Portuguese, "Isso, Isso, Isso", is a reference to a popular Mexican TV show called El Chavo del Ocho about a boy who lives in a barrel.

Freeze Ray is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires a continuous blue laser that freezes enemies. Notes: Has a similar look and basic abilities as the weapon used by Mr. Freeze in various "Batman" media. This gun's flavor text is a reference to Arnold Schwarzenegger's campy performance as Mr. Freeze from the movie Batman & Robin. The synergy "Willing to Sacrifice" is a reference to the 1977 song Cold As Ice by Foreigner.

The Light Gun is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires an incredibly fast zap of white light capable of piercing through enemies. The final bullet in each magazine is a mallard duck, which automatically seeks out enemies to attack them. This duck is capable of piercing once, and has extremely tight homing. Like all other piercing projectiles, the duck's damage will halve after piercing an enemy for the first time. Notes: This gun is a reference to the NES Zapper. The duck being shot is a reference to Duck Hunt, one of the most well-known games to use the NES Zapper.

Raiden Coil is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires a continuous laser that homes in on and damages all on-screen enemies. Notes: Like other guns, this will not fire if the player is up against a wall and trying to shoot into the wall, even though this gun shoots through walls. Backup Gun indirectly doubles this gun's damage due to its direct homing. This gun is a large reference to the Raiden series.

Moonscraper is a gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a laser beam that reflects off of walls or obstacles once. Notes: This gun is a reference to the Moonraker laser rifle from the 1979 James Bond movie Moonraker, as well as the Moonraker Laser weapon from the Nintendo 64 game, GoldenEye 007. The synergy Land Among The Stars references the idiom, "Shoot for the moon; even if you miss, you’ll land among the stars." The synergy Jumping...

Laser Lotus is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires blue laser blasts capable of each piercing 1 enemy. These lasers electrify any water that they pass over. The Laser Lotus does not grant electric immunity, making electrified puddles of water potentially very dangerous to the player. Notes: This gun is a reference to Community. The character Pierce is a "Level 5 Laser Lotus" in his Buddhist renewal community.

H4mmer is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. Effects: Rapid-fires a barrage of bullets. The last shot in every magazine is a high-damage hammer. ETG This hammer has a 20% chance to stun enemies for 3.5 seconds. Notes: This gun may be a reference to the trick of "fanning the hammer", where a single-action revolver is fired by flicking the hammer of the weapon rather than pulling the trigger in o... Bug There is an issue with the Hammer and Nail synergy where if the ammo count exceeds what the base gun would have without the 50% maximum ammo, the current ammo count would reve...

The A.W.P. is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It fires bullets that can pierce through one enemy. As a sniper rifle, its shot speed is extremely fast, and its shots leave lines of smoke. Notes: Due to its slow rate of fire it is easy to fire another gun between A.W.P. shots without A.W.P. downtime. A.W.P is an acronym for Arctic Warfare Police. "Noob Cannon" is a reference to the AWP weapon in Counter-Strike, popular among new and experienced players alike for its ability to one-shot opposing players.

Bullet Bore is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires drills which home in on enemies, stun them, and explode after a short delay. Notes: This gun can hit Cannonbalrog and High Priest during attacks where not normally possible. Helix Bullets will cause the bullets to only shoot and have piercing abilities, without the explosion and stunning effect. The drills fired are able to kill the mines spawned by the Mine Flayer.

Polaris is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Has three different levels, with the gun leveling up after enough enemies are slain. Higher levels deal more damage. Killing 11 enemies will level up Polaris to level 2. Killing 20 more enemies (31 total) will level up Polaris to level 3. Notes: Polaris' level does not affect its selling price. While level 1 and 3 fire a single projectile of varying damage, level 2 fires two. Losing hearts in ways that do not deal damage to the player, such as the Vampire, do not affect the level of Polaris.

Lil' Bomber is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Charges up to fire bouncing, exploding bombs. Like most explosive weapons, this explosion is capable of destroying some enemy bullets. These bombs are uniquely capable of blasting open secret rooms. Deals 30% extra damage to bosses. Notes: Bomberpal and Super Bomberpal stack, allowing Lil' Bomber to fire three bombs at once if the player has both Bomb and Ice Bomb. The King Bomber synergy can provide up to 420 per ammo pickup, or up to 840 with Loot Bag. With both Bomberpal and Super Bomberpal, assuming all three projectiles hit, this number can be up to 1260 per ammo pickup, or 2520 with Loot Bag.

Proton Backpack is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires two slow-moving simultaneous beams; A thick homing (Rad: 18 Vel: 420) red laser that deals very low damage. A thin electric homing (Rad: 15 Vel: 420) beam that deals the majority of the Proton Backpack's DPS. Notes: This gun is a reference to the iconic Proton Pack ghost fighting weapon from the Ghostbusters franchise. The Ammonomicon quote "Crossing Streams" is in reference to the repeated warning in Ghostbusters (1984) "Don't cross the streams", as crossing the streams from multiple Proton Bac...

Jolter is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. It fires large, rectangular bullets with high knockback. Notes: The Jolter is a reference to the Warhammer 40k weapon, the Bolter, typically used by space marines. However, unlike the Bolter, the Jolter isn't an explosive weapon. Having the Demon Head orbital while using the Gun That Can Kill The Past may crash the game and force you to restart the floor. The Synergy "There is Only War" is a reference to Warhammer 40k's iconic tagline: "In the grim darkness of the far future, there is only war."

Pitchfork is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. Effects: Launches fireballs that set enemies on Fire (Dur: 4s DPS: 4). ETG Each fireball can pierce through up to 4 enemies. Notes: Fallen Bullet Kin wield the Pitchfork. CMV Possible Bug The special UI icon for both the Pitchfork and Pitch Perfect in the Chinese Mobile Version of Enter the Gungeon appear blue.

Com4nd0 is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires explosive homing (Rad: Vel: 220) missiles. Notes: This gun is one of the six wielded by the Beholster. When used by the Beholster, it fires homing rockets visually identical to those fired by the player. As it is held by the Beholster, it can be placed on the Beholster Shrine. This gun is based on the real world M202 FLASH rocket launcher.

Hegemony Carbine is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. This weapon rapidly shoots bright, white bolts. Notes: The tagline "All The Same" is possibly a reference to the Rifleman's Creed. This gun is used in The Marine's past. It's possible to bring the Galactic Medal of Valor over to his past, which will allow you to acquire the Decorated synergy.

Tear Jerker is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Shoots tears that pierce enemies and leave puddles of water on collision. The direction of these tears is affected by the player's momentum, meaning that tears angle in the direction the player is moving. Notes: Because the Tear Jerker uses the Water damage type, it deals 2x damage to Muzzle Wisps and Muzzle Flares, and does not ignite Coalers. The Tear Jerker also puts out decorative torches and burning skeletons. This gun and both of its synergies are references to The Binding of Isaac.

AKEY-47 is a gun in Enter the Gungeon that fires keys. Shooting a locked chest or door will unlock it. Alternatively, opening a chest or door with the AKEY-47 equipped will unlock it for free, if the player has no keys. Notes: The AKEY-47 Has identical statistics to the AK-47. Once unlocked, the AKEY-47 may appear for sale in Flynts shop for 3 keys. Its bullets cannot open the padlocks on the trapdoor to the Oubliette and the tunnel to the Resourceful Rat's Lair; however, they can be opened by hand for free with the AKEY-47 e...

Gunderfury is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Gunderfury has multiple forms, which it slowly levels up through as it gains experience over the course of multiple runs. After acquiring enough experience to reach a level up milestone, Gunderfury will instantaneously switch forms. While Gunderfury only changes it's forms at specific level-up milestones, it still progresses through every level between each form. Notes: Rainbow Mode is an effective way to farm experience for Gunderfury, as it is possible to restart until it is acquired in the very first Rainbow Chest.

Gunderfury is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Gunderfury has multiple forms, which it slowly levels up through as it gains experience over the course of multiple runs. After acquiring enough experience to reach a level up milestone, Gunderfury will instantaneously switch forms. While Gunderfury only changes it's forms at specific level-up milestones, it still progresses through every level between each form. Notes: Rainbow Mode is an effective way to farm experience for Gunderfury, as it is possible to restart until it is acquired in the very first Rainbow Chest.

Mimics are a family of enemies in Enter the Gungeon that disguise themselves as scenery, items or containers to catch players unaware. Notes: Brown, Blue, and Green chest mimics wield twin Magnums. Homing bullets, full room damage effects, and companions will ignore mimics that have not yet been revealed. Shooting a mimic that is still hiding to awaken it will not deal any actual damage to the mimic.

The Serpent or Baby Dragun is a secret NPC and companion that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It is part of the secret sequence of events required to fight the Advanced Dragun. Effects: Once acquired, the Serpent will follow the player as an orbital, snaking around them in a wave pattern. The Serpent will block enemy projectiles, similarly to a guon stone. During the High Dragun boss fight, the Serpent will leave the players side- flying back and forth beside the High Dragun periodically spitting an irregular spread of seven enemy b...

Phoenix is a gun in Enter the Gungeon Effects: The muzzle flare of this gun is a fiery ejection that sets enemies caught within on Fire (Dur: 4s DPS: 4). Despite emerging from this fiery belch, the projectile itself does not ignite enemies. Notes: Phoenix's blast of fire does no direct damage, only lighting the enemy on fire. Phoenix Up is likely a reference to the revival item Phoenix Down, from the Final Fantasy series. The shape of the Phoenix is similar to the Shellegun.

Betrayer's Shield is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Upon reloading, creates a protective magical shield on the ground. This shield blocks enemy bullets, and will be destroyed upon taking too much damage. All shields in a room are automatically destroyed when the player leaves it. Notes: The Betrayer's Shield is peculiarly a member of the PISTOL gun class despite almost certainly being more at home among the members of the FULLAUTO class. There are a few bullet patterns in certain boss fights that the shield will not block, such as the large bullets that the Cannonbalrog shoots when he disappears and the line of bu...

Lower Case r is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Fires a burst of 6 letter-shaped projectiles that spell out the word BULLET. While firing, the gun will voice out the word "bullet". Notes: The Lower Case r is voiced by Doseone, the composer of Enter the Gungeons soundtrack. This gun, while obviously a surface level reference to the basic shape of the letter r, may also be a reference to early computer adventure games. The reload animation of the Lower Case r consists of a text cursor deleting the letter with a backspace, and typing an entirely new r.

Gungeon Ant is a gun that alternates between firing spreads of bullets that leave behind oil and ones that burn enemies and ignite oil upon reloading. Notes: This gun is a reference to the real-life Bullet Ant. Known for its extremely painful sting, victims have likened it to "being shot". The Great Queen Ant synergy is a reference to Earthworm Jim, specifically The Evil Queen Pulsating, Bloated, Festering, Sweaty, Pus-filled, Malformed, Slug-for-a-Butt.

Buzzkill is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires sawblades that can ricochet off walls 3 times, and pierce through 2 enemies each. Notes: Buzzkill is slang for someone who ruins fun (in this case, the gun's designer from the Ammonomicon entry), and the gun's name is also a portmanteau of buzzsaw and kill. The pickup text "Sawed On!" is a play on words, based on a "sawed-off shotgun." May be a reference to the Disc Gun in the game Nuclear Throne which also fires ricocheting saw blades.

Stone Dome is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Rapidly fires homing (Rad: 25 Vel: 360) projectiles. While held, any non-boss enemies within 90° of the angle the player is aiming will become afraid, fleeing the player. This effect works over any distance, however enemies will only start to flee the player after coming within a 6 tile range. Notes: The fear status effect can compel enemies to walk backwards into hazards such as dangerous goop or pits. Bug Switching to another gun causes currently afraid enemies to be permanently afraid.

Alien Engine is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Rapidly fires tiny invisible pellets with a very short range, extending only slightly past the gun's visible muzzle flash. Sets enemies on Fire (Dur: 4s DPS: 4). Pushes the player backwards, away from the direction aimed, while firing. Notes: Despite its downsides, this gun has among the highest DPS in the Enter the Gungeon, making it useful for hit-and-run tactics in a Boss battle, or in combination with a longer rang... In September of 2016, Dodge Roll programmer Brent Sodman listed the Alien Engine as his favourite gun in Enter the Gungeon, and Shotgrub as his least favourite.

Rad Gun is a gun in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Passively grants 2 coolness. When reloading the gun, a mark appears on the reload bar, similar to the Cog of Battle. Pressing reload or firing at the right time will increase the gun's damage. The damage dealt by the gun approximately doubles for the first four reloads. Past that, the damage continues to grow infinitely, but at a slower rate. Notes: Refilling the Rad Gun with an Ammo pickup or taking the elevator to the next chamber is considered an automatic reload. Swapping to another weapon for a few seconds will automatically reload the Rad Gun. There is a limit on the maximum reload combo which depends on the reload speed.

Origuni is a gun that fires piercing paper planes that have the Remote Bullets effect and follow the player's crosshair. Notes: Blade Runner - If the player also has Deck4rd, magazine size of both guns is increased by 50%. Paperwork - If the player also has the Ballot or any Table Tech item except for Heat or Shotgun (Blanks, Money, Rage, Rocket, Sight, or Stun), the first shot after reloading will... Paper Lanterns - If the player has Balloon Gun, while holding either gun in a room with enemies, paper lanterns periodically spawn, which drift towards enemies and deal damage. Trivia: The name of the gun comes from origami, the Japanese art of paper-folding.

Ice Breaker is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: The first two shots of each magazine fire a shotgun blast of freezing ice bullets. The final shot of each magazine fires a single large, metal slug that can pierce up to 5 times. This slug will also pass through breakables without being stopped. Notes: Because the ice shots of the Ice Breaker use the Ice damage type, they deal 1.5x damage to Muzzle Wisps and Muzzle Flares, and do not ignite Coalers. Despite the Ammonomicon entry suggesting otherwise, the metal slugs fired by this gun do not deal any more damage to frozen enemies. Prior to the Supply Drop Update, Ice Breaker had 100 maximum ammo.

High Dragunfire is a gun that fires purple bullets resembling flames that have a 1/9 chance to burn enemies. Notes: The secret room containing the High Dragunfire contains six friendly cultists kneeling to a red chest, with various dialogue: First time: "Welcome, fellow acolyte! It appears that Gun Cult fashion has changed substantially since our forefathers were sealed into this holy chamber. After reveal: "Our holy relic... a simple gun that cannot kill the past? Trivia: The High Dragunfire and the cultist's dialogue is a reference to old screenshots of the game, featuring a pink Dragunfire and smaller bullets. The shape of the gun is remarkably similar to that of a Bolter from Warhammer 40,000.

The Sunlight Javelin is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Charges up to throw a fast-moving, highly accurate thunderbolt that deals considerable damage and sets enemies on Fire (Dur: 10s DPS: 4). Lasting for 10 seconds, this is the longest lasting non-green fire effect that can be applied by any weapon in Enter the Gungeon. Fire inflicted by the Sunlight Javelin has a unique, solar appearance. Notes: This gun is an excellent room clearing weapon, as it one-shots most enemies even into the Forge, and is extremely accurate and ammo-efficient. This gun can be reloaded immediately after firing to activate Hip Holster if above 100 ammo.

Shotbow is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires shotgun spreads of 6 crossbow bolts at once. Notes: The Shotbow is in the SHOTGUN gun class and as such benefits from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. This gun's description could be a reference to the episode Tuvix, from the TV show Star Trek Voyager, where two characters were merged into one as a result of a transporter (a sor... It could also be a reference to the short story by George Langelaan titled "The Fly" originally published in the June 1957 issue of Playboy magazine, which inspired two films of t...

Dungeon Eagle is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It can be charged to fire a more powerful shot, or tap fired to shoot more frequent, weaker projectiles. Notes: The name Dungeon Eagle is a reference to the real-world 'Desert Eagle' gun. The description of the weapon refers to the Desert Eagle's popularity in American culture and the weapon's iconic reputation despite its shortcomings. With Don't Hoot the Messenger, the reload animation of the gun shows its slide and barrel rotating 360 degrees in reference to the real-world ability of owls to rotate their heads...

Magnum is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon, as well as in Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead under a different name. Notes: The Magnum is the most commonly used enemy weapon in the Gungeon, being held by Bullet Kin, Veteran Bullet Kin, Minelets, Cardinals, Shroomers, Ashen Bullet Kin, Mutant Bullet Kin... The quote "5 shots or 6?" is a reference to the film Dirty Harry. The gun's description also is a reference to Harry calling his magnum a filthy gun.

Smiley's Revolver is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Decreases shop prices by 15% while in the players inventory. Notes: This item's description references both the name and effect of Disarming Personality. The entry for Smiley's Revolver in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of Smiley.

Smiley's Revolver is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Decreases shop prices by 15% while in the players inventory. Notes: This item's description references both the name and effect of Disarming Personality. The entry for Smiley's Revolver in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of Smiley.

Smiley's Revolver is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Decreases shop prices by 15% while in the players inventory. Notes: This item's description references both the name and effect of Disarming Personality. The entry for Smiley's Revolver in the Physical Ammonomicon has a small doodle of Smiley.

Not to be confused with the Devolver. Evolver is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Has 6 different forms, starting as an amoeba and progressively evolving into more complex organisms. Notes: Enemies do not need to be killed by Evolver's bullets for it to evolve. When Evolver evolves, its ammo will be fully replenished. The flavor text "Tamagunchi" is a reference to the digital pet brand Tamagotchi. These digital pets progress through different life stages as they grow, much like the Evolver.

Shell is a Gun in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon which fires a spread of 3 shotguns, each of which fires 2 bullets upon hitting an enemy or obstacle. Notes: This gun seems to be in reference to two colloquial sayings that mean relatively the same thing - "Firing a gun" and "Firing a shell". The smaller bullets fired by launched shotgun are not affected by the player's Damage stat. They are however affected by Bullet Modifiers and items that adjust projectile damage directly such as Cursed Bullets or Gilded Bullets.

High Kaliber is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Consumes 6 ammo per shot, effectively having an ammo capacity of 111. Fires fat white maggots that can pierce once. On non-fatally piercing through an enemy, that enemy is marked and the projectile is converted into a spinning 'soul', continuing along its initial trajectory. Notes: Souls cannot be stepped on during the player's I-frames after taking damage. Stepping on a bosses soul can damage them even while they are in normally un-damageable states; While Blobulord is split into smaller Blobulon-shaped bullets.

"She #&%#’n finished it! It is OP as hell." - Dodge Roll The Finished Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It entirely replaces the Unfinished Gun once the Ammonomicon has been completed. Effects: Fires black and white bullets that are each capable of bouncing up to twice; each time the bullet bounces, the damage will be multiplied by 1.5. The final shot in each cylinder is a larger, piercing bullet that reflects enemy bullets it comes near as it travels, but does not bounce. Reflected bullets each deal 10 damage, and are sent in the same direction as the main shot.

Regular Shotgun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon, Exit the Gungeon, and Enter the Gungeon: House of the Gundead. Notes: The Regular Shotgun has an equip animation in which the gungeoneer racks the pump. ETG The Regular Shotgun is in the SHOTGUN gun class, meaning it benefits from the Shotgun Affinity synergy. The Regular Shotgun is the gun wielded by Red Shotgun Kin, Ashen Shotgun Kin, and Mutant Shotgun Kin.

Unfinished Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires piercing projectiles. Notes: Despite not actually igniting enemies, the Unfinished Gun is in the FIRE gun class. As a consequence, having the Unfinished Gun in the player's inventory makes them less likely to find other guns in the FIRE class that actually burn enemies. The Blacksmith can be seen working on an Unfinished Gun.

Unfinished Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. It fires piercing projectiles. Notes: Despite not actually igniting enemies, the Unfinished Gun is in the FIRE gun class. As a consequence, having the Unfinished Gun in the player's inventory makes them less likely to find other guns in the FIRE class that actually burn enemies. The Blacksmith can be seen working on an Unfinished Gun.

The Exotic is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Fires an explosive missile that can pierce up to 10 enemies. While held, grants a small robotic companion that blocks bullets and reveals the contents of nearby chests. If the player has revealed the contents of a chest, the contents of that chest will be permanently locked in as that item. Notes: The Exotic is a reference to the exotic-rarity rocket launcher Gjallarhorn from Destiny, one of the most beloved and memetic exotics in the game. The robot companion is a reference to the Ghosts from Destiny.

The Marine Sidearm is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It serves as The Marine's starting gun in both games. It has a good accuracy and a stronger knockback than other starting guns. Notes: Cannot be dropped. Is technically automatic, as tapping the fire button does not provide a faster fire rate than holding it down. After defeating The Marine's past with his alternate skin, an alternate skin of this weapon is unlocked.

The Rusty Sidearm is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It serves as The Hunters starting gun in both games. Notes: Cannot be dropped. Cannot be obtained by any Gungeoneer other than The Hunter or The Paradox. After defeating The Hunter's past with her alternate skin, an alternate skin for this gun is unlocked.

The Dart Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It serves as The Cultists starting gun in both games. Notes: The Dart Gun may be a reference to the Nerf Maverick REV-6, a toy foam dart blaster that has the same shape, similar color pattern (albeit purple instead of yellow), and also hold... The Dart Gun is a replacement toy for the Cultist, after they lost their previous toy blaster (the Light Gun) down a pit. The alternate skin of this gun resembles a worm, in order to match the Cultists apple alt skin.

Robot's Right Hand is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. It serves as The Robot's starter weapon in both games. Notes: Cannot be dropped. Cannot be obtained by any Gungeoneer other than The Robot or The Paradox. Robot's Right Hand has the largest magazine size of the starting guns.

Blasphemy is a Gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon, where it functions as the starting weapon of The Bullet. Effects: Fires a piercing rainbow sword projectile when swung. ETG This projectile is only spawned if the player is at full health. The Robot is never considered at full health, and as such, cannot fire the sword projecitle. When swung, Blasphemy creates a wide arc that destroys enemy bullets caught within.

Magazine Rack is a rechargeable active item. Effects: Creates a pool of infinite ammo for a short time. Standing inside of Magazine Rack's radius while holding an empty gun will not let you shoot using that gun. The gun must have at least 1 ammo for this to work. Notes: Just Shoot Me - If the player has Camera, this shoots at nearby enemies while active. Junk Mail - If the player has Mailbox, activating the Magazine Rack causes Mailbox to only shoot out packages. Magazine Clip - If the player has Drum Clip, Magazine Rack's radius doubles. Trivia: The Just Shoot Me synergy may refer to the sitcom "Just Shoot Me!" where the characters work at a fashion magazine and photograph models.

Lich's Eye Bullets is a passive item. The Gunslinger starts with this item. Effects: Makes all guns act as if they have all of their synergies with other items or guns. Notes: Certain synergies will not be simulated with Lich's Eye Bullets, or have additional requirements for being simulated. Some two-component synergies that require both guns/items to function, as opposed to one or the other simply being in the player's inventory, will not be simulated. Dual-wielding synergies such as The Submachinist will not be simulated. Trivia: Possible reference to Clint Eastwood's "Deadeye" as the Lich is the lord over the dead. Nod to the fact that The Gunslinger & Lich both have bullets as prosthetic eyes.

Trank Gun is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Notes: This gun is wielded by Beadies, Coalers, and the Beholster. As it is held by the Beholster, it can be placed on the Beholster Shrine. The Trank Gun is a reference to the Tranq Gun from the Metal Gear franchise, in particular a modified Beretta M9 used in Metal Gear Solid: The Twin Snakes and Metal Gear Solid 2...

Cat Bullet King Throne is a passive item. Effects: Grants flight. Shoots bullets around the player upon dodge rolling. This has a 2-second cooldown. The bullets benefit from all bullet modifiers. Notes: Mad Cats - If the player is holding 3rd Party Controller the bullets shot from Cat Bullet King Throne home in on the enemy. Usurper - If the player has Crown of Guns, the Crown appears to fire more rapidly and also makes the Crown automatically charm Bullet Kin and Veteran Bullet Kin when held. Bullets are larger while The Scrambler is equipped.

Baby Good Shelleton is a passive item and companion that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: Follows the player and attacks enemies with a green laser. The laser does 12.5 DPS for 3.86s, with the downtime between lasers being 4.9s. The Baby Good Shelleton deals 5.5 DPS overall. Notes: If the player stands outside the Bullet King's boss door, the Baby Good Shelleton may kill the Chancellor. The description of the item is an altered version of the Shelleton's Ammonomicon entry.

Glass Cannon is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Charges up to shoot a powerful piercing bolt of light. This projectile ignores the Boss DPS cap. If the player takes damage while holding the Glass Cannon it will shatter, losing all its ammunition. Notes: While shattered (empty due to taking damage), the Glass Cannon has no sprite, effectively making it invisible. If the Glass Cannon is emptied by using up its ammo reserve instead of taking damage, the gun remains unshattered and can be dropped and retrieved like any other. Unlike the Balloon Gun, it's not possible to drop the weapon while it's shattered. This also makes selling it to the Sell Creep impossible in this state.

Shadow Clone is an active item. Effects: Upon use, spawns a temporary clone of the player. The clone's movements are opposite to the player. The clone will shoot with the same weapon the player has equipped, and aims where the player aims. Notes: Kinjutsu - If the player also has Decoy, Shadow Clone spawns three clones. Due to the nature of this synergy, it requires an inventory-expanding item such as Utility Belt or Backpack. Multiple clones can be spawned at the same time. Trivia: This item is referring to Naruto Uzumaki's favored technique from the namesake anime series Naruto.[1] The words "Believe it!" in the item description are a direct reference to his catchphrase early on in the English dubbed series.

Scouter is a passive item in Enter the Gungeon. Effects: Shows enemy health bars. Dealing damage to enemies will trigger a number pop-up showing how much damage was dealt. Decimal damage numbers will be rounded to the nearest whole integer in their pop-up. The killing blow dealt to an enemy will only show a damage number equal to the amount of HP it had remaining, not the actual maximum damage the attack could have done. Notes: Mini bosses will not show their health bar. The Scouter can make spotting the original Killithid when they split easy as it will still show the damage dealt to the original. This item is a reference to similar technology in the television series Dragon Ball Z.

Katana Bullets is a passive item and a bullet upgrade. Effects: Increases curse by 1. Upon killing an enemy, bullets have a chance to unleash a flurry of slashes along the path of the original bullet, damaging enemies. If an enemy is killed with a beam style gun, Katana Bullets has a 100% chance to trigger. Notes: Because the flurry pushes enemies and players around, using Katana Bullets can damage the player in indirect and unpredictable ways. Companions that damage enemies can trigger the effects of Katana Bullets. The blade-flurries can appear behind enemies and push them into the player. Trivia: A Katana is a type of traditional curved Japanese sword, renowned for their sharpness and cutting power.

Wood Beam is a gun that appears in Enter the Gungeon and Exit the Gungeon. Effects: While the trigger is held, this gun will extend in the form of a stiff beam until reaching the end of its range. Swinging the beam into enemies deals considerably more damage and knockback than direct hits. The beam deals 1 damage per second when used to hit directly like a normal beam weapon. Notes: The Wood Beam deals no damage to chests. For this reason, it can be an excellent way to check for Mimics. Wood Beam's damage multiplier from swing speed applies after Unity's flat damage bonus.
